subjects.summary: Before major projects are built, environmental impact assessments evaluate how the project might affect ecosystems, water, air, and communities.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Symbolism and Allegory
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Reading strand: symbolism uses an object or image to represent a deeper idea, while an allegory is an entire story in which characters and events represent broader meanings, often moral or political.

quiz.q: An allegory is best described as ___.
options: An entire story where characters and events represent broader meanings

quiz.q: Symbolism differs from allegory in that symbolism ___.
options: Usually involves a specific object or image representing an idea, rather than an entire story

quiz.q: Which is an example of a common literary symbol?
Why might an author write an entire story as an allegory?
options: To convey a broader moral, political, or social message through the narrative
To make a story purely literal with no symbolism

quiz.q: Why is understanding symbolism and allegory considered an advanced reading skill?
options: It requires no interpretation at all
These devices never appear in complex texts
Symbolism and allegory are the simplest literary devices to identify
It requires recognizing deeper meaning beyond the literal events of a story

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Simplifying and Expanding Algebraic Expressions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Algebra strand: simplifying an expression means combining like terms, while expanding involves using the distributive property to remove parentheses, such as 2(x + 3) becoming 2x + 6.

quiz.q: Simplify: 3x + 5x.
Expand: 2(x + 3).
Simplify: 4x + 2y minus x + 3y.
Like terms are terms that ___.
options: Have the same variable raised to the same power

quiz.q: Expand: 5(2x + 4).

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Cell Division and Growth
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Life Systems strand: cell division allows organisms to grow, repair tissue, and reproduce, with mitosis producing two identical cells from one original cell.

quiz.q: Cell division allows organisms to ___.
Mitosis results in ___.
options: Two identical cells from one original cell
The complete destruction of a cell

quiz.q: Why is cell division important for healing an injury, such as a cut?
options: Cell division has no role in healing
New cells are produced to repair and replace damaged tissue
Injuries heal with no biological process involved
Cell division only occurs before birth

quiz.q: Which process allows a single-celled organism to reproduce?
Why do organisms need a reliable process like cell division to grow?
options: Growth never involves cells at all
Cell division only occurs in plants, not animals
Organisms do not actually grow over time
Growth requires producing new cells to increase the size and number of tissues

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Great Depression and Its Impact on Canada
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: the Great Depression of the 1930s brought severe economic hardship to Canada, including widespread unemployment, leading to major changes in government policy and social programs.

quiz.q: The Great Depression occurred mainly during which decade?
A major effect of the Great Depression in Canada was ___.
options: A booming economy with no challenges

quiz.q: The hardships of the Great Depression led to changes in ___.
Why is the Great Depression considered a turning point in Canadian social policy?
options: It had no connection to unemployment or hardship
It had no lasting effect on government policy
It occurred after all major social programs were created
It highlighted the need for government support systems during economic crises

quiz.q: Which is an example of hardship faced by many Canadians during the Great Depression?
options: Unlimited access to jobs and wealth

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Literary Analysis Essay
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: a literary analysis essay closely examines elements of a text, such as character, theme, or symbolism, supporting a clear interpretation with evidence from the text.

quiz.q: A literary analysis essay focuses on ___.
options: Only summarizing the plot with no analysis
Closely examining elements of a text with supporting evidence

quiz.q: Which is an example of a literary element that might be analyzed in an essay?
Why is textual evidence important in a literary analysis essay?
options: Evidence makes an analysis less convincing
It supports the writer’s interpretation with specific examples from the text
Evidence is unnecessary in literary analysis
A literary analysis should never reference the text

quiz.q: A strong thesis in a literary analysis essay should ___.
options: Avoid taking any position at all
Simply restate the plot with no interpretation
Present a clear, arguable interpretation of the text

quiz.q: Why might different readers write different literary analyses of the same text?
options: Literary analysis allows for no personal interpretation
Interpretation can vary based on the reader’s perspective and the evidence they emphasize
Texts only ever have one single possible meaning
Every reader must always reach the exact same interpretation

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Solving Multi-Step Equations
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Algebra strand: solving a multi-step equation involves combining like terms, using the distributive property if needed, and then applying inverse operations to isolate the variable.

quiz.q: Solve for x: 2x + 3x + 5 = 25.
Solve for y: 3(y + 2) = 21.
When solving a multi-step equation, which step is often completed first?
options: Ignoring one side of the equation
Combining like terms or expanding parentheses

quiz.q: Solve for n: 4n minus 2n + 6 = 16.
Solve for m: 2(m + 4) minus 3 = 13.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Photosynthesis and Cellular Respiration
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Life Systems strand: photosynthesis converts sunlight into stored energy in plants, while cellular respiration releases that stored energy for cells to use, forming a connected cycle.

quiz.q: Photosynthesis converts ___ into stored chemical energy.
Cellular respiration releases energy stored in ___.
Photosynthesis and cellular respiration are considered connected because ___.
options: They have no relationship to each other
The products of one process are often used as inputs for the other

quiz.q: Which gas is taken in by plants during photosynthesis?
Why is cellular respiration essential for living cells?
options: It provides the usable energy cells need to carry out their functions
Only photosynthesis provides usable energy for cells
Cells never require energy to function
Cellular respiration has no role in providing energy

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada in World War II
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: Canada played a significant role in World War II, contributing troops, resources, and industrial production, which also led to major social changes at home.

quiz.q: Canada contributed to World War II through ___.
options: Only diplomatic letters with no other support
A single soldier with no broader involvement

quiz.q: World War II led to significant social changes in Canada, such as ___.
options: Increased roles for women in the workforce
A complete halt to all industry
The end of all international relationships

quiz.q: Why is Canada’s role in World War II considered historically significant?
options: Canada was not involved in World War II
It had no lasting impact on Canada
It occurred before World War I
It contributed to shaping Canada’s international reputation and domestic society

quiz.q: Canadian industry during World War II shifted to focus on ___.
options: Goods entirely unrelated to the war
Only agricultural products with no industrial output
Producing nothing of use to the war effort

quiz.q: Why do historians study the domestic changes in Canada during World War II?
options: Domestic changes during the war had no lasting significance
Canadian society remained completely unchanged throughout the war
These changes have no connection to Canadian history
To understand how wartime needs reshaped Canadian society and the economy

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Subjunctive Mood and Conditional Sentences
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: the subjunctive mood expresses wishes, hypotheticals, or conditions contrary to fact, often seen in sentences like If I were taller, I would reach the shelf.

quiz.q: The subjunctive mood is often used to express ___.
options: Wishes, hypotheticals, or conditions contrary to fact

quiz.q: Which sentence correctly uses the subjunctive mood?
options: If I will be taller, I would reach the shelf.
If I was taller, I would reach the shelf.
If I were taller, I would reach the shelf.
If I am taller, I would reach the shelf.

quiz.q: A conditional sentence typically includes ___.
options: An if clause and a result clause

quiz.q: Which sentence expresses a wish using the subjunctive mood?
options: I wish I was is on vacation right now.
I wish I am on vacation right now.
I wish I were on vacation right now.

quiz.q: Why might writers use the subjunctive mood in formal or literary writing?
options: To express hypothetical or unreal situations clearly
It replaces the need for verbs entirely
It is only used for factual statements
The subjunctive mood has no specific grammatical purpose

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Direct and Partial Variation
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Algebra strand: direct variation describes a relationship where one quantity is a constant multiple of another (y equals kx), while partial variation includes a fixed starting amount plus a variable rate (y equals kx plus b).

quiz.q: In direct variation, the relationship between x and y can be written as ___.
In partial variation, the relationship between x and y can be written as ___.
options: y equals x divided by k

quiz.q: Which situation is an example of direct variation?
options: The total cost of apples, based only on price per apple times quantity, with no extra fee
A taxi fare with a flat starting fee plus a per-kilometre rate
A fixed monthly fee with no other charges
A situation with no relationship between two quantities

quiz.q: Which situation is an example of partial variation?
options: Buying items where total cost is exactly price times quantity, with no starting fee
A phone plan with a flat monthly fee plus a per-text charge
A situation where cost is always exactly zero
A relationship with no constant rate at all

quiz.q: In the equation y equals 3x plus 5, what does the 5 represent in a partial variation context?
options: An unrelated constant with no meaning

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Plate Tectonics and Continental Drift
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: plate tectonics explains how Earth’s crust is divided into large plates that move slowly over time, a process related to the theory of continental drift.

quiz.q: Plate tectonics explains how Earth’s crust is divided into ___.
options: Large plates that move slowly over time

quiz.q: Continental drift is the theory that ___.
options: Earth’s crust has no movement at all
Continents have moved and shifted position over millions of years
Continents have always remained in the exact same position

quiz.q: Movement of tectonic plates can result in ___.

options: Matching coastlines and fossil patterns across separated continents
A complete absence of fossil evidence
Continents that have never been connected in any way

quiz.q: Why is understanding plate tectonics important for studying natural disasters?
options: Natural disasters occur with no geological cause
It helps explain the causes behind events like earthquakes and volcanic eruptions
Plate tectonics only affects ocean currents
Plate tectonics has no connection to natural disasters

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Cold War and Canada’s Role
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: during the Cold War, Canada aligned with Western allies, participated in organizations like NATO, and played a role in international tensions between the United States and the Soviet Union.

quiz.q: During the Cold War, Canada generally aligned with ___.
NATO is an organization that Canada participated in during ___.
options: A time with no international alliances

quiz.q: The Cold War was primarily a period of tension between the United States and ___.

options: To align with allies and contribute to collective international security
Canada avoided all international involvement during this period
Alliances provided no benefit to Canada
Canada had no role in international relations at that time

quiz.q: Why is Canada’s role during the Cold War studied as part of Canadian history?
options: It shows how Canada positioned itself within global political tensions
Canada was entirely disconnected from world events during the Cold War
Canada had no role in Cold War events
This period has no relevance to Canadian history

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Connotation, Denotation, and Word Choice
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Language strand: denotation is a word’s literal meaning, connotation is the feeling or association it carries, and skilled writers choose words carefully based on both to create a desired effect.

quiz.q: Denotation refers to ___.
Connotation refers to ___.
options: The number of syllables in a word
The feeling or association a word carries beyond its literal meaning

quiz.q: Why might a writer choose the word slender instead of skinny, despite similar denotations?
options: Connotation has no effect on writing
Slender often carries a more positive connotation
The words have completely different denotations

quiz.q: Which word likely has a more negative connotation than confident, despite a similar meaning?

options: Word choice can strongly influence how an audience feels about a topic
Persuasive writing should avoid considering word choice
Connotation has no role in persuasion

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Similar Triangles and Scale Factor
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Geometry strand: similar triangles have the same shape but not necessarily the same size, with corresponding sides in proportion according to a consistent scale factor.

quiz.q: Similar triangles have ___.
options: No relationship to each other at all
The same shape but not necessarily the same size

quiz.q: If two triangles are similar with a scale factor of 2, and one side of the smaller triangle is 5 cm, the corresponding side of the larger triangle is ___.
A scale factor describes ___.
options: The ratio between corresponding sides of similar shapes
The area of a triangle only

quiz.q: In similar triangles, corresponding angles are ___.
If a triangle has sides of 3, 4, and 5, and is scaled by a factor of 3, what are the new side lengths?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Optics: Refraction and Lenses
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Matter and Energy strand: refraction is the bending of light as it passes between materials of different densities, a principle used in lenses to focus or spread out light.

quiz.q: Refraction occurs when light ___.
options: Bends as it passes between materials of different densities
Always travels in a perfectly straight line with no change

quiz.q: A lens uses refraction to ___.
options: Prevent any light from passing through

quiz.q: A convex lens generally causes light rays to ___.
options: Always spread apart with no convergence

quiz.q: Why does a straw in a glass of water sometimes appear bent?
options: Light refracts differently as it passes from air into water
The straw physically changes shape underwater
Water has no effect on light

quiz.q: Which everyday device relies on lenses and refraction to function?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Quebec’s Quiet Revolution and Canadian Federalism
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: the Quiet Revolution was a period of significant social, political, and economic change in Quebec during the 1960s, which influenced discussions about Canadian federalism.

quiz.q: The Quiet Revolution took place mainly during which decade?
The Quiet Revolution brought significant change to which Canadian province?
The Quiet Revolution involved changes in areas such as ___.
options: Social, political, and economic life in Quebec

quiz.q: Why is the Quiet Revolution significant to discussions about Canadian federalism?
options: It had no connection to Canadian federalism
It ended all discussion of provincial and federal relationships
It shaped ongoing conversations about the relationship between Quebec and the rest of Canada
It was unrelated to Quebec’s political development

quiz.q: Canadian federalism refers to ___.
options: A single, unified government with no divisions of power
A system with no provincial governments at all
A concept unrelated to Canadian government structure
The division of powers and responsibilities between federal and provincial governments

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Comparing Themes Across Genres
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Reading strand: comparing how a similar theme, such as courage or friendship, is explored differently across genres like poetry, fiction, and nonfiction reveals how form shapes meaning.

quiz.q: Comparing themes across genres involves examining ___.
options: Only one single text in isolation
How a similar theme is explored differently across different types of texts
Themes that never appear in more than one genre
Nothing related to meaning or form

quiz.q: Which is an example of comparing a theme across genres?
options: Reading only one book and ignoring all others
Examining how courage is portrayed in a poem versus a nonfiction article
Comparing two unrelated topics with no shared theme

quiz.q: Why might a theme be expressed differently in poetry compared to nonfiction?
options: Poetry never contains any identifiable themes
All genres express themes in exactly the same way
Genre has no connection to how a theme is presented
Different genres use distinct techniques, such as imagery or structure, to convey ideas

quiz.q: Why is comparing themes across genres considered a valuable literacy skill?
options: It replaces the need to understand any individual text
It deepens understanding of how form and structure shape meaning
Only one genre can ever explore meaningful themes
This comparison has no educational value

quiz.q: Which pairing would be useful for comparing how a theme like resilience is explored across genres?
options: A blank page with no content
A poem about resilience and a nonfiction article about resilience

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Surface Area and Volume of Cylinders
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Geometry strand: the surface area of a cylinder includes its two circular bases plus the curved surface, while its volume is found by multiplying the area of its circular base by its height.

quiz.q: The volume of a cylinder is found using the formula ___.
A cylinder’s surface area includes ___.
options: Two circular bases plus the curved side surface

quiz.q: If a cylinder’s circular base area is 20 square units and its height is 6 units, what is its volume?
Why might engineers need to calculate the volume of a cylindrical tank?
options: To determine how much liquid or material the tank can hold
Volume calculations are unnecessary for tanks
Only surface area matters for tanks, never volume
Cylindrical tanks have no measurable volume

quiz.q: A cylinder’s two circular bases are ___ in size.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Chemical Reactions: Types and Balancing
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Matter and Energy strand: chemical reactions can be classified into types such as synthesis and decomposition, and balancing a chemical equation ensures the same number of atoms appears on both sides.

quiz.q: A synthesis reaction occurs when ___.
options: Two or more substances combine to form a new substance
A single substance breaks apart into multiple substances

quiz.q: A decomposition reaction occurs when ___.
options: Substances combine to form something new
A single substance breaks down into two or more simpler substances

quiz.q: Balancing a chemical equation ensures that ___.
options: No atoms are involved in the reaction
The same number of atoms appears on both sides of the equation
The number of atoms can differ freely between sides

quiz.q: Why is it important for a chemical equation to be balanced?
options: It simply makes the equation look neater with no real purpose
Chemical equations are never actually balanced in real reactions

quiz.q: Which is an example of a synthesis reaction?
options: A compound breaking into its individual elements
Two elements combining to form a compound

subjects.subject: SocialStudies

quiz.q: The Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms became part of the Constitution in ___.

options: Freedom of expression and equality rights

quiz.q: Legal rights protected under the Charter include protections related to ___.
options: No connection to the justice system

quiz.q: Why is the Charter considered a significant part of Canadian law?
options: It establishes constitutionally protected rights and freedoms for people in Canada
It applies to no one in Canada
It was created before Canada became a country

quiz.q: Equality rights under the Charter are intended to protect against ___.
options: Discrimination based on factors like race or gender
Discrimination that is fully permitted by law
Nothing related to fairness or discrimination

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Reflective Personal Essay
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: a reflective personal essay explores a meaningful experience from the writer’s life and considers its lasting impact, insight, or lesson learned.

quiz.q: A reflective personal essay focuses on ___.
options: A meaningful experience and its lasting impact or lesson
Only listing unrelated facts with no personal connection

quiz.q: Why is insight or reflection important in this type of essay?
options: It replaces the need to describe the experience
Reflection has no value in personal writing
It shows deeper thinking about what the experience meant to the writer
A reflective essay should avoid any personal insight

quiz.q: Which is an example of reflective insight in a personal essay?
options: That challenge taught me to be more patient with myself and others.
I woke up. I ate breakfast. I went to school.
The weather was cold that day.
This essay has no connection to real experiences.

quiz.q: A strong reflective essay often connects a specific experience to ___.
options: Only a list of facts with no interpretation
An unrelated topic with no connection
A broader lesson or understanding about life

quiz.q: Why might writers choose to write reflective personal essays?
options: This type of writing avoids any personal connection
Personal reflection serves no purpose in writing

subjects.subject: Math

quiz.q: If a coin is flipped and a die is rolled, what is the probability of getting heads and rolling a 6?

options: Divide one probability by the other

subjects.subject: Science

quiz.q: Ohm’s Law is expressed as ___.

quiz.q: In a circuit, resistance refers to ___.
options: Opposition to the flow of electric current

quiz.q: If voltage increases while resistance stays the same, current will generally ___.

quiz.q: Why is Ohm’s Law useful for understanding electrical circuits?
options: Ohm’s Law has no practical use in circuits
It helps predict how changes in voltage or resistance affect current flow
It describes relationships unrelated to electricity
It only applies to circuits with no current

subjects.subject: SocialStudies

quiz.q: Critics of globalization often raise concerns about ___.
options: Globalization having no effect on any country
Only benefits, with no drawbacks discussed

quiz.q: One concern about globalization is that it can lead to ___.
options: Complete economic isolation for every country
The loss of local industries in some regions
The growth of local industries with no downsides

quiz.q: Why might some communities feel negatively impacted by globalization?
options: Globalization has no effect on local communities
Increased international competition can affect local jobs and industries
All communities benefit equally with no downsides

quiz.q: Supporters and critics of globalization often disagree about ___.
options: Topics unrelated to trade or economics
Whether its benefits outweigh its potential downsides

quiz.q: Why is it useful to consider multiple perspectives on globalization?
options: Globalization affects every country in an identical way
It provides a more complete understanding of its complex effects
Considering multiple perspectives has no value
Only one perspective on globalization is ever valid

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Analyzing Propaganda Techniques
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Media Literacy strand: propaganda uses techniques such as name-calling, bandwagon appeal, and emotional language to influence public opinion, often simplifying complex issues.

quiz.q: Propaganda is designed to ___.
options: Influence public opinion, often through persuasive or misleading techniques
Provide a fully balanced view of every issue

quiz.q: Bandwagon appeal is a propaganda technique that suggests ___.
options: You should do something because many other people are doing it
No one else agrees with the message
Facts are irrelevant to any argument
The audience should ignore popular opinion entirely

quiz.q: Name-calling as a propaganda technique involves ___.
options: Avoiding any judgment of people or ideas
Using negative labels to discredit an opposing person or idea

quiz.q: Why might propaganda oversimplify complex issues?
options: Propaganda always presents highly detailed, nuanced arguments
To make a message easier to spread and more emotionally persuasive

quiz.q: Why is it useful for citizens to recognize propaganda techniques?
options: Recognizing these techniques serves no purpose
Citizens should accept persuasive messages without question
It helps them think critically about messages instead of being easily influenced
Propaganda has no real influence on public opinion

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Compound Interest
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Financial Literacy strand: compound interest is calculated on both the original principal and any interest already earned, causing savings or debt to grow faster than with simple interest over time.

quiz.q: Compound interest is calculated based on ___.
options: The original amount plus any interest already earned
Only the original amount, with no interest added

quiz.q: Why does compound interest typically result in more growth over time than simple interest?
options: Interest rates are automatically lower with compound interest
Interest is earned on previously earned interest as well as the original amount
Compound interest works exactly the same way as simple interest
Compound interest always results in less money over time

quiz.q: If $100 earns 10 percent compound interest in the first year, how much interest is earned in year one?
In year two of compound interest, interest is calculated on ___.
options: The original amount plus year one’s interest
Only the original amount from year one

quiz.q: Why is understanding compound interest useful when considering long-term savings or loans?
options: It only applies to very small amounts of money
It helps predict how savings or debt will grow significantly over time
Compound interest has no real-world financial impact
Compound interest calculations are never used in real finance

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Nitrogen Cycle
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Life Systems strand: the nitrogen cycle describes how nitrogen moves between the atmosphere, soil, and living things, with bacteria playing a key role in converting nitrogen into forms plants can use.

quiz.q: The nitrogen cycle describes how nitrogen moves between ___.
options: The atmosphere, soil, and living things

quiz.q: Which organisms play a key role in converting nitrogen into forms plants can use?
options: No organisms are involved in this process

quiz.q: Nitrogen makes up a large portion of Earth’s ___.
Why is nitrogen important for plant growth?
options: Plants never require nitrogen in any form
Nitrogen has no role in plant growth
Plants need nitrogen in usable forms to build proteins and grow

quiz.q: Why is the nitrogen cycle considered an important natural process?
options: This cycle only affects non-living matter
Nitrogen is not actually found in nature
It supports life by making nitrogen available in forms living things can use
The nitrogen cycle has no ecological importance

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Indigenous Self-Government and Modern Treaties
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: many Indigenous communities in Canada today exercise self-government through modern treaties and agreements that recognize their authority over their own affairs.

quiz.q: Self-government for Indigenous communities generally refers to ___.
options: Having no say in their own affairs
Being governed entirely by another country
Having no recognized rights at all
Having authority to govern their own community affairs

quiz.q: A modern treaty between a government and an Indigenous community often addresses ___.
Why is self-government important to many Indigenous communities?
options: It has no connection to community wellbeing
It removes all rights from Indigenous peoples
Self-government has no real impact on communities
It allows communities to make decisions that reflect their own needs, values, and traditions

quiz.q: Modern treaties differ from many historical treaties in that they are often ___.
options: Identical to treaties signed centuries ago
Negotiated to address more contemporary rights and governance issues

quiz.q: Learning about Indigenous self-government helps students understand ___.
options: That Indigenous governance ended in the past
That Indigenous communities have no role in modern Canada
How Indigenous nations exercise rights and responsibilities today
That treaties are no longer relevant

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Using Passive Voice Effectively
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: while active voice is often clearer, passive voice can be used effectively when the focus should be on the action or receiver rather than who performed it, such as in scientific writing.

quiz.q: Passive voice can be effective when ___.
options: Clarity is not a goal of the sentence
The focus should be on the action or receiver rather than the doer
A sentence should always hide all information
The subject must always be stated first

quiz.q: Which sentence uses passive voice effectively in a scientific context?
options: I heated the solution to 100 degrees Celsius.
The solution was heated to 100 degrees Celsius.

quiz.q: Why might scientific writing often use passive voice?
options: Passive voice makes scientific writing less accurate
To focus on the process or results rather than who performed the action
Passive voice is never used in scientific writing
Scientific writing should always use active voice exclusively

quiz.q: Which is a potential drawback of overusing passive voice?
options: There are no drawbacks to passive voice
Passive voice always makes writing clearer
Sentences can become less direct or clear

quiz.q: Which sentence is written in active voice?
options: The ball was thrown by Sam.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Sampling and Bias in Data Collection
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Data Management strand: a sample should represent the larger population fairly, and bias in sampling occurs when certain groups are overrepresented or underrepresented, leading to inaccurate conclusions.

quiz.q: A sample in statistics refers to ___.
options: A smaller group selected to represent a larger population
A single random individual with no connection to a study
Data that has no connection to any population

quiz.q: Bias in sampling occurs when ___.
options: Certain groups are overrepresented or underrepresented
No data is collected at all
Sampling always produces perfectly accurate results
Every group is represented in exact proportion to the population

quiz.q: Why might surveying only students in one specific class about a school-wide issue introduce bias?
options: A single class always represents the whole school perfectly
Every class always has identical opinions
That group might not accurately represent the opinions of the entire school

quiz.q: A random sample is generally considered more reliable because ___.
options: It always excludes certain groups intentionally
Randomness always leads to biased results
It never accurately represents a population
It gives every member of the population a fair chance of being included

quiz.q: Why is recognizing bias in data collection important?
options: Sampling bias is impossible to identify
Biased data can lead to inaccurate or misleading conclusions
Bias only affects opinions, never factual data
Bias has no effect on the accuracy of conclusions

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Astronomy: Galaxies and the Universe
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: a galaxy is a massive collection of stars, gas, and dust held together by gravity, and our solar system exists within the Milky Way galaxy, one of billions of galaxies in the universe.

quiz.q: A galaxy is best described as ___.
options: A massive collection of stars, gas, and dust held together by gravity

quiz.q: Our solar system exists within which galaxy?
Scientists estimate that the universe contains ___.
What force holds a galaxy’s stars and matter together?
Why do astronomers study galaxies beyond our own?
options: Studying other galaxies serves no scientific purpose
Galaxies beyond our own have no connection to astronomy
To better understand the structure, formation, and scale of the universe
Only our own galaxy is worth studying

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Urban Planning and Sustainable Cities
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: urban planning involves designing cities to balance housing, transportation, and green space, with sustainable cities aiming to reduce environmental impact while supporting growing populations.

quiz.q: Urban planning involves designing cities to balance ___.
options: Only building height with no other factors

quiz.q: A sustainable city aims to ___.
options: Reduce environmental impact while supporting its population

quiz.q: Which is an example of sustainable urban planning?
options: Increasing pollution with no mitigation efforts
Expanding public transportation to reduce car dependency
Removing all parks and green spaces

quiz.q: Why might urban planners prioritize green space in city design?
options: Urban planners never consider environmental factors
Green space always harms a city’s development
Green space has no benefits for a city
Green space can improve air quality and quality of life for residents

quiz.q: Why is sustainable urban planning becoming increasingly important?
options: Growing populations and environmental concerns require thoughtful, balanced city design
Population growth has no effect on city planning needs
Urban planning has no connection to environmental issues
Cities never need to consider sustainability

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Formal Analytical Report
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: a formal analytical report investigates a topic in depth, presenting findings with objective, well-organized evidence and a clear structure, often used in academic or professional contexts.

quiz.q: A formal analytical report typically presents ___.
options: Objective, well-organized evidence on a topic
Only the writer’s personal opinions with no evidence

quiz.q: Why is clear organization important in an analytical report?
options: It helps readers follow and understand the findings logically
Reports should always be presented with no clear structure
Organization has no effect on how a report is understood
Clear organization makes a report less credible

quiz.q: Which is a feature commonly found in a formal analytical report?
options: Headings that organize information by topic
No structure or headings of any kind

quiz.q: Why might an analytical report avoid overly casual language?
options: Language choice has no effect on a report’s tone
Formal language helps maintain credibility in an academic or professional context
Reports are never expected to sound professional

quiz.q: An analytical report’s conclusion should typically ___.
options: Summarize key findings and their significance
Contain no connection to the report’s findings

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Slope and Rate of Change
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Algebra strand: slope represents the rate of change between two variables on a graph, calculated as the vertical change (rise) divided by the horizontal change (run) between two points.

quiz.q: Slope is calculated as ___.
If a line rises 6 units and runs 3 units, what is its slope?
A steeper line on a graph generally indicates ___.
options: No rate of change at all

quiz.q: A horizontal line has a slope of ___.
Why is slope a useful concept when analyzing graphs of real-world situations, such as speed over time?
options: It represents how quickly one quantity changes in relation to another
Rate of change cannot be represented graphically
Slope only applies to graphs with no practical meaning

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Environmental Toxicology and Pollution
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: environmental toxicology studies how pollutants, such as chemicals released into air, water, or soil, affect living organisms and ecosystems.

quiz.q: Environmental toxicology studies how ___.
options: Pollutants affect living organisms and ecosystems
Living things have no connection to their environment
Pollution never has any measurable effects
Only weather patterns are studied, not pollution

quiz.q: Which is an example of a pollutant that could affect an ecosystem?
Pollution entering a water source can affect ___.
options: Only the colour of the water, with no other effects
Both aquatic organisms and the people who rely on that water
Nothing at all, since water is unaffected by pollutants

quiz.q: Why might scientists monitor pollution levels in ecosystems?
options: Monitoring pollution serves no scientific purpose
Pollution levels never change over time
Ecosystems are never affected by pollution
To assess risks to wildlife and human health and guide environmental protection

quiz.q: Which action could help reduce pollution’s impact on an ecosystem?
options: Releasing untreated chemicals directly into rivers
Properly treating wastewater before it enters natural water sources
Increasing the use of harmful chemicals with no regulation

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Human Migration Patterns and Push-Pull Factors
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: human migration is often influenced by push factors, which encourage people to leave a place, and pull factors, which attract people to a new location.

quiz.q: A push factor in migration is something that ___.
options: Attracts people to a new location
Has no influence on migration decisions
Encourages people to leave a place
Only applies to animals, never humans

quiz.q: A pull factor in migration is something that ___.
options: Attracts people to a new location
Discourages people from leaving a location

quiz.q: Which is an example of a push factor?
options: Conflict or economic hardship in a region
A strong existing economy at home
Job opportunities in a new country
Political stability in the current location

quiz.q: Which is an example of a pull factor?
options: Better job opportunities in a new location

quiz.q: Why is it useful to study push and pull factors when learning about migration?
options: They help explain the reasons behind people’s decisions to move
Migration always occurs with no identifiable reasons
These factors have no connection to migration patterns
Push and pull factors only apply to a single historical era

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Evaluating Rhetorical Appeals
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Reading strand: rhetorical appeals include ethos (credibility), pathos (emotion), and logos (logic), and identifying which appeals a text relies on helps readers evaluate how persuasion is being used.

quiz.q: Ethos as a rhetorical appeal relies on ___.
options: The credibility or trustworthiness of the speaker

quiz.q: Pathos as a rhetorical appeal relies on ___.
options: Only statistics with no emotional language

quiz.q: Logos as a rhetorical appeal relies on ___.
Which is an example of an appeal to ethos?
options: A doctor citing their medical expertise to support a health claim
A story designed only to make the audience feel sad

quiz.q: Why is it useful for readers to identify which rhetorical appeals a text uses?
options: All persuasive texts use identical rhetorical strategies
Rhetorical appeals have no effect on persuasion
Identifying appeals is not a useful reading skill
It helps them critically evaluate how the text is attempting to persuade them

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Number Theory: GCF and LCM
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Number strand: the greatest common factor (GCF) is the largest number that divides evenly into two or more numbers, while the least common multiple (LCM) is the smallest number that is a multiple of two or more numbers.

quiz.q: What is the GCF of 12 and 18?
What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
The GCF of two numbers is ___.
options: The largest number that divides evenly into both
Always equal to one of the two numbers
The smallest number that is a multiple of both

quiz.q: The LCM of two numbers is ___.
options: The largest number that divides evenly into both
The smallest number that is a multiple of both

quiz.q: What is the GCF of 20 and 30?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Scientific Literacy: Evaluating Scientific Claims
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Inquiry strand: evaluating a scientific claim involves checking whether it is supported by reliable evidence, tested through valid methods, and reviewed by other experts.

quiz.q: A reliable scientific claim is typically supported by ___.
options: Claims that cannot be tested in any way
Evidence gathered through valid, testable methods

quiz.q: Why is peer review important in evaluating scientific claims?
options: Peer review has no role in science
It allows other experts to check the accuracy and validity of the findings
Scientific claims should never be reviewed by anyone
Peer review always makes findings less accurate

quiz.q: Which is a warning sign that a scientific claim might be unreliable?
options: It is supported by multiple independent studies
It is based on carefully controlled experiments
It lacks supporting evidence or has never been tested
It has been reviewed by other scientists

quiz.q: Why might scientists want to repeat an experiment before fully accepting a claim?
options: Repeating results helps confirm that findings are consistent and reliable
Repeating experiments has no scientific value
A single experiment is always considered sufficient proof
Reliable claims never need to be tested more than once

quiz.q: Why is scientific literacy an important skill for evaluating information in daily life?
options: Scientific literacy has no real-world application
All claims presented as scientific are automatically true
It helps people critically assess whether claims are based on sound evidence
Evaluating claims is unnecessary in everyday life

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Comparative Government Systems
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: comparing different government systems around the world, such as parliamentary democracies, republics, and monarchies, highlights different approaches to governance and representation.

quiz.q: A parliamentary democracy is a system where ___.
options: A single ruler has unlimited, unchecked power
No elections take place at all
Citizens elect representatives who form a governing body

quiz.q: A republic is generally a system where ___.
options: The head of state is typically elected rather than hereditary
Only a king or queen can ever lead
There is no government structure at all
Citizens have no role in choosing leaders

quiz.q: Comparing government systems across countries helps students understand ___.
options: That all countries are governed identically
That government systems have no real differences
Different ways nations organize decision-making and representation
That comparison of government types is unnecessary

quiz.q: Why might different countries choose different systems of government?
options: All countries are required to use the same system
Government systems often reflect a country’s unique history, values, and needs
Government systems have no connection to history or culture
Choice of government system is always random

quiz.q: Studying comparative government systems can help citizens ___.
options: Assume only one system has ever existed
Better understand how their own government compares to others
Avoid learning about their own government

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Unreliable Narrators
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Reading strand: an unreliable narrator is a storyteller whose account may be biased, mistaken, or dishonest, requiring readers to critically evaluate what is really happening in the story.

quiz.q: An unreliable narrator is one whose account may be ___.
options: Impossible to include in a story
Written by someone other than the author

quiz.q: Why might an author choose to use an unreliable narrator?
options: To create suspense or challenge readers to question what is really happening
It always makes a story less interesting
It removes the need for any plot
Unreliable narrators are never used in literature

quiz.q: Which is a clue that a narrator might be unreliable?
options: A story with no narrator present
A narrator who never says anything at all
A narrator who is always proven completely correct
Inconsistencies between their account and other details in the story

quiz.q: Reading critically when a narrator seems unreliable involves ___.
options: Assuming the story has no deeper meaning
Questioning the narrator’s claims and looking for supporting or contradicting evidence

quiz.q: Why is recognizing an unreliable narrator considered an advanced reading skill?
options: It requires careful analysis of tone, consistency, and perspective
This skill has no connection to comprehension
It requires no critical thinking at all
Unreliable narrators are always obvious immediately

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Systems of Two Linear Relations: A Graphical Introduction
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Algebra strand: a system of two linear relations can be solved graphically by finding the point where their two lines intersect, representing the values that satisfy both relations.

quiz.q: The solution to a system of two linear relations, solved graphically, is ___.
options: The origin of the graph in every case
The point where the two lines intersect
Any point on either line separately

quiz.q: If two lines on a graph never intersect, the system has ___.
options: A solution at the origin only

quiz.q: If two lines on a graph are identical, the system has ___.
options: A solution only at the origin

quiz.q: Why is finding the point of intersection useful when solving a system of linear relations?
options: It has no meaning in the context of the two relations
It represents the values of the variables that satisfy both relations at once
It only matters for one of the two lines
Intersection points are unrelated to solving systems

quiz.q: Graphing two linear relations on the same grid allows you to ___.
options: Prevent any comparison between the relations
Visually identify where their solutions overlap
Remove the need for any calculations

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Endocrine System: Hormones and Regulation
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Human Body Systems strand: the endocrine system uses glands to release hormones into the bloodstream, regulating processes like growth, metabolism, and stress response.

quiz.q: The endocrine system regulates the body using ___.
Hormones are produced by structures called ___.
Which of these processes might the endocrine system help regulate?
How does the endocrine system typically differ from the nervous system in how it sends signals?
options: It has no way of sending any signals
It always works faster than the nervous system
It relies on hormones travelling through the bloodstream rather than fast electrical signals
It has no connection to body regulation

quiz.q: Why is the endocrine system important for maintaining balance in the body?
options: It affects only external appearance, not internal processes
The body functions with no hormonal regulation at all
It helps regulate many internal processes needed for healthy functioning
The endocrine system has no role in maintaining balance

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Vietnam War and Its Global Impact
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: the Vietnam War, fought primarily during the 1960s and 1970s, was a significant Cold War-era conflict that had lasting political and social effects around the world, including in Canada.

quiz.q: The Vietnam War took place primarily during which decades?
The Vietnam War is often considered connected to which broader global conflict?
The Vietnam War had lasting effects on ___.
options: Political and social attitudes in many countries, including Canada
Nations with no involvement or connection to the war

quiz.q: Why is the Vietnam War often studied in the context of Cold War history?
options: It was completely unrelated to global politics
It reflected broader tensions between opposing political systems during that era
It had no connection to Cold War tensions
It occurred before the Cold War began

quiz.q: Why might a war fought primarily in one country still have a significant global impact?
options: Global impact is impossible from a single regional conflict
International alliances and public opinion can be affected far beyond the location of the conflict
Wars never have any impact beyond their immediate location
Only wars fought on multiple continents matter internationally

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a Satirical Piece
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: satire uses humour, irony, or exaggeration to criticize or draw attention to flaws in society, people, or ideas.

quiz.q: Satire is a form of writing that uses ___.
options: Purely factual reporting with no commentary
No persuasive or critical elements at all
Humour, irony, or exaggeration to critique something

quiz.q: Why might a writer choose satire instead of a direct, serious critique?
options: Direct critique is always more effective than satire
Humour and exaggeration can make a critique more engaging or memorable
Satire removes all meaning from a piece of writing

quiz.q: Which is an example of a technique commonly used in satire?
options: Avoiding any criticism of the subject
Removing all humour from the piece
Exaggerating a flaw to make it seem absurd

quiz.q: Satire is often used to draw attention to ___.
options: Only positive qualities with no criticism
Flaws in society, people, or ideas
Random, unrelated topics with no purpose

quiz.q: Why might readers need to think carefully when reading satire?
options: Satire requires no interpretation at all
Readers never need to consider tone in satire
Satire is always completely literal with no hidden meaning
The exaggerated or ironic tone might not be immediately obvious

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Exponent Laws: Multiplying and Dividing Powers
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Number strand: when multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents; when dividing powers with the same base, subtract the exponents.

quiz.q: Simplify: x cubed times x squared.
Simplify: x to the fifth power divided by x squared.
When multiplying powers with the same base, you should ___.
When dividing powers with the same base, you should ___.
Simplify: 2 to the fourth power times 2 to the third power.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Biomes of the World
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: biomes are large regions characterized by distinct climate and communities of plants and animals adapted to those conditions, such as deserts, rainforests, and tundra.

quiz.q: A biome is best described as ___.
options: A large region with a distinct climate and community of organisms

quiz.q: Which biome is characterized by very low precipitation and extreme temperatures?
The tropical rainforest biome is known for its ___.
options: High biodiversity and warm, wet climate

quiz.q: The tundra biome is characterized mainly by ___.
options: Cold temperatures and a lack of trees

quiz.q: Why do organisms in different biomes have different adaptations?
options: They must adjust to the specific climate and conditions of their biome
All biomes have identical conditions requiring no adaptation
Organisms never adapt to their environment
Adaptations have no connection to climate

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Fall of the Berlin Wall and the End of the Cold War
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: the fall of the Berlin Wall in 1989 symbolized the end of the Cold War era, leading to significant political changes in Europe, including German reunification.

quiz.q: The Berlin Wall fell in which year?
The fall of the Berlin Wall is often seen as symbolizing ___.
options: The end of the Cold War era
The beginning of World War II
The start of the Cold War
The founding of the United Nations

quiz.q: Following the fall of the Berlin Wall, Germany experienced ___.
options: A return to conflict with no resolution
No political change of any kind

quiz.q: Why is the fall of the Berlin Wall considered a major turning point in world history?
options: It caused the Cold War to begin
It marked significant political change and reduced Cold War tensions in Europe
It had no lasting political significance
It was unrelated to Cold War tensions

quiz.q: The Berlin Wall had originally divided the city of Berlin into ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Gerunds and Infinitives
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: a gerund is a verb form ending in -ing used as a noun (Swimming is fun), while an infinitive is the base form of a verb preceded by to (I want to swim).

quiz.q: A gerund is a verb form that ___.
options: Is never used in a sentence
Ends in -ing and is used as a noun
Always begins with the word to

quiz.q: An infinitive is typically formed by ___.
options: Placing to before the base form of a verb

quiz.q: Which sentence contains a gerund?
Which sentence contains an infinitive?
Why might a writer choose to use a gerund instead of a regular noun?
options: Gerunds always replace the need for a subject
A gerund can add a sense of ongoing action to a sentence
There is no functional difference between a gerund and any noun

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Volume and Surface Area of Composite 3D Shapes
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Geometry strand: a composite 3D shape is made up of two or more basic solids combined, and its volume or surface area can be found by breaking it into simpler shapes and adding or subtracting their measurements.

quiz.q: A composite 3D shape is made up of ___.
options: Two or more basic solids combined

quiz.q: To find the volume of a composite shape, you can ___.
options: Break it into simpler shapes and add their volumes
Ignore parts of the shape entirely

quiz.q: If a composite shape is made of a rectangular prism with volume 40 cubic units and a triangular prism with volume 15 cubic units attached, what is the total volume?
Why might a real-world object, like a house-shaped storage shed, be considered a composite shape?
options: A shed has no identifiable geometric shape
It combines multiple basic shapes, such as a rectangular prism and a triangular prism roof
It is made of only one simple shape
Composite shapes never appear in real life

quiz.q: When calculating the surface area of a composite shape, it is important to ___.
options: Only measure the shape’s volume, not surface area
Account for any internal faces that are not part of the outer surface
Assume all composite shapes have identical surface areas

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Chemical Safety and WHMIS Symbols
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Matter and Energy strand: WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) symbols communicate important safety information about hazardous materials, helping people handle chemicals safely.

quiz.q: WHMIS stands for ___.
options: World Health and Medical Information Standard
Weather Hazard Measurement and Identification System
A system unrelated to chemical safety

quiz.q: WHMIS symbols are designed to ___.
options: Replace the need for any safety training
Communicate important safety information about hazardous materials

quiz.q: Why is it important to recognize WHMIS symbols in a lab or workplace setting?
options: They help people understand risks and handle materials safely
These symbols have no practical safety purpose
Safety symbols are never used in real workplaces

quiz.q: A WHMIS symbol indicating a flammable material would likely warn about ___.
options: A material unrelated to fire hazards
The risk of the material catching fire
A material that is completely safe to burn

quiz.q: Why might students learn about WHMIS symbols as part of science class?
options: Chemical safety has no connection to science education
Safety information is unnecessary in a classroom setting
To build safe habits when working with materials in labs and other settings
WHMIS symbols are only relevant to a single profession

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Apartheid and the Struggle for Equality in South Africa
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: apartheid was a system of racial segregation and discrimination in South Africa, and its end in the early 1990s followed decades of resistance and advocacy for equality.

quiz.q: Apartheid was a system of ___.
options: A system unrelated to South Africa
A government with no policies on race

quiz.q: Apartheid was officially practiced in which country?
Apartheid came to an end in the early ___.
The end of apartheid followed decades of ___.
options: Complete indifference from the global community

quiz.q: Why is the history of apartheid studied as part of world history?
options: It illustrates the impact of systemic discrimination and the importance of the struggle for equality
It has no connection to global discussions on equality

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Technical and Academic Vocabulary
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Language strand: technical and academic vocabulary consists of specialized words used within specific subjects or fields, and understanding these terms is important for reading and writing in those areas.

quiz.q: Technical vocabulary refers to ___.
options: Specialized words used within specific subjects or fields
Words used only in casual conversation
Vocabulary that never appears in academic writing

quiz.q: Why is understanding academic vocabulary important for reading textbooks?
options: Understanding vocabulary is unnecessary for comprehension
It helps readers understand subject-specific concepts accurately
Academic vocabulary has no connection to understanding a subject

quiz.q: Which is an example of technical vocabulary specific to science?
Why might a student need to learn new vocabulary when starting a new subject, like geometry?
options: New subjects never require any new vocabulary
New subjects often introduce specialized terms essential to understanding key concepts
Vocabulary has no connection to understanding a subject
Technical vocabulary is identical across every subject

quiz.q: A glossary in a textbook is useful because it ___.
options: Replaces the need to read the textbook itself
Provides definitions for specialized or technical terms used in the text

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Data: Outliers and Their Effect on Statistics
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Data Management strand: an outlier is a data value that is significantly higher or lower than the rest of a data set, and it can strongly affect statistics like the mean.

quiz.q: An outlier is a data value that is ___.
options: Significantly higher or lower than the rest of the data set
The same as every other value in the set
Always exactly equal to the mean
Impossible to identify in any data set

quiz.q: Which statistic is most affected by an outlier?
options: The mode, in most typical cases
Only the median, and never the mean

quiz.q: In the data set 2, 3, 4, 5, 50, which value is the outlier?
Why might statisticians examine a data set for outliers before calculating the mean?
options: An outlier can skew the mean and make it less representative of the typical values
The mean should always be calculated without checking for outliers
Outliers should never be considered in statistics
Outliers have no effect on any calculation

quiz.q: Compared to the mean, the median is generally ___ affected by outliers.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Robotics and Automation in Technology
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science and Technology strand: robotics involves designing machines that can perform tasks automatically, often using sensors and programming to interact with their environment.

quiz.q: Robotics involves designing machines that can ___.
options: Only operate manually with no automation

quiz.q: Sensors in a robot are used to ___.
options: Replace the need for any programming
Have no role in robotic function
Prevent the robot from functioning at all
Detect and respond to information from the environment

quiz.q: Automation refers to ___.
options: A process with no connection to technology
Tasks that always require constant human control
Machines that can never repeat a task
Technology performing tasks with little or no human intervention

quiz.q: Which is an example of a real-world application of robotics?
options: A basic hand tool with no automation
A drawing made without any technology
A robotic arm assembling products in a factory
A book with no technological components

quiz.q: Why is understanding robotics increasingly relevant in today’s world?
options: Robotics has no connection to modern technology
Robots are used exclusively in fictional stories
Automation and robotics are used across many industries and everyday technologies

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The United Nations and Peacekeeping
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: United Nations peacekeeping missions involve international forces working to maintain or restore peace in regions affected by conflict, with Canada historically playing a notable role.

quiz.q: United Nations peacekeeping missions aim to ___.
options: Maintain or restore peace in regions affected by conflict
Avoid any involvement in international conflicts

quiz.q: Peacekeeping forces are typically made up of ___.
Canada has historically been recognized for its role in ___.
options: A role unrelated to international peace efforts

quiz.q: Why might the United Nations organize peacekeeping missions instead of relying on a single country to act alone?
options: International cooperation can bring broader legitimacy and shared resources to peace efforts
Peacekeeping missions never require cooperation between countries
International cooperation has no benefit in peacekeeping efforts
A single country always resolves conflicts more effectively alone

quiz.q: Why is learning about peacekeeping relevant to understanding international relations?
options: Peacekeeping missions have never actually taken place
Conflicts are never addressed through international cooperation
It shows how countries can work together to address global conflicts
Peacekeeping has no connection to international relations

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Evaluating Counterarguments in Persuasive Texts
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Reading strand: a counterargument acknowledges an opposing viewpoint, and evaluating how effectively a writer addresses it helps readers judge the overall strength of a persuasive text.

quiz.q: A counterargument in persuasive writing is used to ___.
options: Remove the writer’s original argument entirely

quiz.q: Why might a writer include a counterargument in a persuasive essay?
options: Including a counterargument always weakens an essay
Counterarguments should never appear in persuasive writing
To show they have considered other perspectives, which can strengthen their credibility
It replaces the need for the writer’s own argument

quiz.q: An effective response to a counterargument typically ___.
options: Explains why the writer’s position still holds despite the opposing view
Ignores the counterargument completely after mentioning it
Simply restates the counterargument with no response

quiz.q: Why is evaluating how a writer handles counterarguments useful for readers?
options: This evaluation has no value for readers
It helps readers judge how well-reasoned and balanced the argument truly is
Readers should never question a persuasive text’s reasoning
Counterarguments are never relevant to understanding an argument

quiz.q: Which is an example of effectively addressing a counterargument?
options: Changing the topic entirely to avoid the opposing view
Agreeing completely with the opposing view with no further explanation
Refusing to mention any opposing views
Acknowledging the opposing view, then explaining why the evidence still supports the writer’s position

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Geometry: Angle Relationships in Polygons
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Geometry strand: the sum of interior angles in a polygon can be calculated using the formula (n minus 2) times 180 degrees, where n represents the number of sides.

quiz.q: What is the sum of interior angles in a triangle?
What is the sum of interior angles in a quadrilateral?
The formula for the sum of interior angles in a polygon is ___.
What is the sum of interior angles in a pentagon (5 sides)?
Why is the interior angle sum formula useful when working with polygons?
options: Angle sums are always identical for every polygon regardless of sides
It has no practical use in geometry
It allows you to calculate missing angles without measuring each one directly

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Respiratory and Circulatory Systems
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Human Body Systems strand: the respiratory system brings oxygen into the body and removes carbon dioxide, while the circulatory system transports that oxygen and other nutrients throughout the body.

quiz.q: The main role of the respiratory system is to ___.
options: Bring oxygen into the body and remove carbon dioxide

quiz.q: The main role of the circulatory system is to ___.
options: Transport oxygen and nutrients throughout the body

quiz.q: The heart is the main organ of the ___ system.
The lungs are the main organs of the ___ system.
How do the respiratory and circulatory systems work together?
options: The circulatory system brings in air directly
The respiratory system pumps blood throughout the body
They function with no connection to each other
Oxygen brought in by the respiratory system is transported through the body by the circulatory system

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Multiculturalism Policy in Depth
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: Canada became the first country to adopt an official multiculturalism policy in 1971, aiming to recognize and support the cultural diversity of its population.

quiz.q: Canada adopted an official multiculturalism policy in which year?
Canada’s multiculturalism policy was notable for making Canada ___.
options: The last country to consider cultural diversity
The first country to adopt an official multiculturalism policy
A country with no cultural diversity at all
The only country ever to have immigrants

quiz.q: The goal of Canada’s multiculturalism policy is to ___.
options: Require all citizens to share an identical culture
Recognize and support the cultural diversity of its population
Ignore the cultural backgrounds of Canadians

quiz.q: Why might Canada’s multiculturalism policy be seen as connected to its identity as a nation?
options: The policy was created with no consideration of Canadian history
It reflects Canada’s history of immigration and diverse population
It has no connection to Canada’s history or identity
Canada has never had a diverse population

quiz.q: Why is studying multiculturalism policy relevant to understanding modern Canada?
options: Multiculturalism has no connection to Canadian identity
The policy has never influenced Canadian society
This policy has no relevance to modern Canadian society
It helps explain how Canada approaches diversity and inclusion as a country

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a Speech for a Specific Audience
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: writing an effective speech requires considering the specific audience’s interests, knowledge, and expectations to choose appropriate language, tone, and content.

quiz.q: Writing a speech for a specific audience requires considering ___.
options: The audience’s interests, knowledge, and expectations
Nothing about who will be listening

quiz.q: Why might a speech for young children use different language than a speech for adults?
options: Language should be appropriate to the audience’s age and understanding
All speeches should use identical vocabulary regardless of audience
Audience consideration has no effect on speech writing
Language never needs to change based on audience

quiz.q: Which is an example of adapting a speech for a specific audience?
options: Avoiding any connection to the audience’s interests
Using relevant examples the audience is likely to understand and connect with
Using only complex vocabulary regardless of the audience
Ignoring who will be listening to the speech

quiz.q: Why is tone an important consideration when writing a speech for a particular audience?
options: Speeches should always sound exactly the same regardless of audience
Tone only matters in written essays, not speeches
The right tone helps the message resonate and be well received
Tone has no effect on how a speech is received

quiz.q: Considering audience expectations can help a speech writer decide ___.
options: That audience has no connection to speech writing
Nothing useful about how to write the speech
What content and structure will be most effective and appropriate
To ignore the purpose of the speech entirely

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Financial Literacy: Currency Exchange and Conversion
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Financial Literacy strand: currency exchange involves converting money from one currency to another using an exchange rate, which represents how much one currency is worth compared to another.

quiz.q: An exchange rate represents ___.
options: How much one currency is worth compared to another
The total amount of money in a country
Only how much tax is charged
A fixed amount that never changes

quiz.q: If the exchange rate is 1 Canadian dollar equals 0.75 US dollars, how many US dollars would you get for 100 Canadian dollars?
Why might exchange rates change over time?
options: They are influenced by economic factors that can shift regularly
Exchange rates always stay exactly the same forever
Currency values never change under any circumstances
Exchange rates have no connection to economics

quiz.q: Why is understanding currency exchange useful when travelling to another country?
options: It helps travellers understand the real value and cost of items in a foreign currency
Currency exchange has no relevance when travelling
Understanding exchange rates serves no practical purpose
All countries use the exact same currency value

quiz.q: If 1 Canadian dollar equals 1.5 units of another currency, how many units would 20 Canadian dollars convert to?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Renewable vs. Non-Renewable Resources: Global Perspectives
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Matter and Energy strand: countries around the world rely differently on renewable resources, like solar and wind, and non-renewable resources, like coal and oil, based on geography, technology, and economic factors.

quiz.q: A country’s reliance on renewable versus non-renewable resources can depend on ___.
options: Nothing related to its location or economy
Only the size of the country, with no other influence
Its geography, technology, and economic factors

quiz.q: Which is an example of a renewable resource used differently around the world?
options: A resource that never varies by region
Solar energy, which is more effective in sunnier regions

quiz.q: Non-renewable resources, such as oil and coal, are considered limited because they ___.
options: Are available in unlimited supply everywhere
Take an extremely long time to form and can eventually run out

quiz.q: Why might some countries invest more heavily in renewable energy than others?
options: Every country has identical access to every energy source
Renewable energy investment is unrelated to geography or economics
Available natural resources, technology, and economic priorities can vary by country
All countries rely on the exact same energy sources

quiz.q: Why is understanding global resource use important when studying environmental science?
options: Global resource use has no connection to environmental science
All countries use resources in an identical way
This topic has no real-world relevance
It helps explain differences in energy policy and environmental impact around the world

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Trade Agreements and Their Effects on Canada
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: trade agreements between countries establish rules for buying and selling goods and services, affecting Canada’s economy, industries, and international relationships.

quiz.q: A trade agreement is best described as ___.
options: A rule that prevents all trade between countries
An agreement establishing rules for trade between countries
An agreement unrelated to buying or selling goods
A policy that only applies within a single country

quiz.q: Trade agreements can affect Canada’s ___.
Why might Canada choose to enter into a trade agreement with another country?
options: They have no connection to international relationships
Trade agreements are illegal for Canada to enter
To create more favourable conditions for trade, such as reduced tariffs
Trade agreements provide no economic benefit

quiz.q: Which is a potential effect of a trade agreement on a specific Canadian industry?
options: Increased access to new markets or increased competition
The complete elimination of that industry with no other outcome
A guaranteed benefit with no possible drawbacks
No effect on any industry whatsoever

quiz.q: Why is it useful for citizens to understand how trade agreements affect their economy?
options: Citizens do not need to understand economic policy
It helps them understand economic changes that may affect jobs and prices
Trade agreements have no connection to daily economic life
Trade agreements never have any real-world effects

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Analyzing Documentary Techniques
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Media Literacy strand: documentaries use techniques such as interviews, narration, and selective editing to present information, and recognizing these choices helps viewers evaluate a documentary’s perspective.

quiz.q: Documentaries often use techniques such as ___.
options: Only fictional dialogue with no factual basis
Random unrelated footage with no structure

quiz.q: Selective editing in a documentary can ___.
options: Shape how viewers understand the topic being presented
Remove the need for any narration
Always present a fully neutral, unbiased account
Have no effect on how information is perceived

quiz.q: Why might a documentary filmmaker choose to include specific interviews?
options: To support the perspective or argument the documentary is presenting
Interviews always represent every possible viewpoint equally
Interviews have no connection to a documentary’s message
Interviews are never included in documentaries

quiz.q: Why is it valuable for viewers to consider a documentary’s perspective critically?
options: Critical thinking is unnecessary when watching documentaries
Documentaries never involve any creative choices
Even factual media can present information selectively to support a particular viewpoint
All documentaries present completely neutral, unbiased information

quiz.q: Narration in a documentary is used to ___.
options: Remove all context from the footage
Replace the need for any visual content
Confuse the viewer intentionally with no purpose
Guide viewers’ understanding of the events and information shown

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Rational and Irrational Numbers: An Introduction
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Number strand: a rational number can be written as a fraction of two integers, while an irrational number, such as pi, cannot be expressed exactly as a simple fraction.

quiz.q: A rational number can be expressed as ___.
options: A number that never terminates or repeats
Only a whole number with no fractions allowed

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of an irrational number?
An irrational number, when written as a decimal, is ___.
options: Always able to be written as a simple fraction

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a rational number?
Why might the square root of a non-perfect-square number, like the square root of 7, be considered irrational?
options: All square roots are automatically rational
It can always be written as a simple fraction
It is always a whole number
It cannot be expressed exactly as a simple fraction and its decimal never terminates or repeats

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Immune System and Disease Prevention
