subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Human Body Systems strand: the immune system defends the body against pathogens, and practices like vaccination, handwashing, and proper nutrition can help support the immune system and prevent disease.

quiz.q: The immune system’s main role is to ___.
options: Defend the body against harmful pathogens

quiz.q: Which is a practice that can help prevent the spread of disease?
Vaccines work by helping the immune system ___.
options: Recognize and respond more quickly to specific pathogens in the future

quiz.q: Proper nutrition can support the immune system by ___.
options: Providing the body with resources needed for healthy immune function
Having no connection to immune health
Replacing the need for any immune response

quiz.q: Why is understanding disease prevention considered important for public health?
options: Preventing illness has no connection to community wellbeing
Public health has no relationship to individual health practices
Disease prevention has no effect on public health
Preventive practices can help reduce the spread of illness within communities

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Refugee Crises and International Response
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: a refugee crisis occurs when large numbers of people are forced to flee their home country due to conflict or persecution, often requiring coordinated international humanitarian response.

quiz.q: A refugee is generally someone who ___.
options: Has never left their home country
Has no connection to conflict or persecution
Has been forced to flee their home country due to conflict or persecution
Chooses to move for purely recreational reasons

quiz.q: A refugee crisis often requires ___.
options: Complete indifference from the global community
A single country acting entirely alone with no support

quiz.q: Which organization might be involved in responding to a refugee crisis?
options: An organization with no connection to humanitarian issues
A group uninvolved in international affairs

quiz.q: Why might countries choose to accept refugees during a crisis?
options: Refugee crises have no connection to international cooperation
To provide safety and support for people fleeing dangerous conditions
Accepting refugees provides no humanitarian benefit
Countries are never involved in refugee resettlement

quiz.q: Why is understanding refugee crises important in studying global issues?
options: It highlights the human impact of conflict and the importance of international cooperation
Refugee crises never require any international attention
This topic has no relevance to social studies
Refugee crises have no connection to global issues

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Correcting Dangling and Misplaced Modifiers
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: a dangling modifier lacks a clear word to describe, while a misplaced modifier is positioned too far from the word it describes, both of which can create confusing or unintended meanings.

quiz.q: A dangling modifier is a modifier that ___.
options: Has no clear word in the sentence to describe
Is always placed correctly in a sentence

quiz.q: A misplaced modifier is one that is ___.
options: Positioned too far from the word it is meant to describe
Always placed correctly next to the word it describes
Only found at the beginning of a sentence

quiz.q: Which sentence contains a dangling modifier? Running quickly, the finish line came into view.
options: The finish line was near the runner.
She ran quickly toward the finish line.
The runner crossed the finish line quickly.

quiz.q: Why is it important to fix dangling and misplaced modifiers in writing?
options: These issues never affect a sentence’s meaning
To avoid confusing or unintended meanings in a sentence
Clarity has no connection to modifier placement
Modifiers should always be placed randomly

quiz.q: Which is a corrected version of a sentence with a misplaced modifier? Wearing a red jacket, I saw my friend.
options: Wearing a red jacket, my friend I saw.
I saw my friend wearing a red jacket.
Both versions mean the exact same thing with no ambiguity.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Solving Equations with Variables on Both Sides
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Algebra strand: solving an equation with variables on both sides involves first combining variable terms onto one side, then using inverse operations to isolate the variable.

quiz.q: Solve for x: 3x + 4 = x + 10.
Solve for y: 5y minus 2 = 2y + 7.
When solving an equation with variables on both sides, the first step is often to ___.
options: Ignore one side of the equation entirely
Combine the variable terms onto one side

quiz.q: Solve for n: 4n + 6 = 2n + 16.
Solve for m: 6m minus 3 = 3m + 12.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Space Exploration: Missions and Technology
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: space exploration relies on missions and technologies such as rockets, satellites, and space stations to study space and expand human understanding of the universe.

quiz.q: Space missions often rely on technologies such as ___.
A space station allows astronauts to ___.
options: Immediately return to Earth after a few minutes
Live and conduct research in space for extended periods

quiz.q: Satellites launched into space are often used for ___.
options: Only entertainment, with no other function

quiz.q: Why is space exploration considered valuable to scientific understanding?
options: Space missions never gather any useful information
It has no connection to expanding human knowledge
Space exploration provides no scientific benefit
It expands knowledge about the universe, other planets, and Earth itself

quiz.q: Which is an example of a real space exploration technology?
options: A device with no connection to space exploration
A tool used only for underwater exploration
A vehicle designed only for use on Earth’s roads
A robotic rover exploring the surface of Mars

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Technology’s Impact on Modern Society and Government
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: technology has significantly influenced modern society and government, affecting communication, access to information, and how citizens engage with political processes.

quiz.q: Technology has significantly influenced modern society by affecting ___.
options: Only entertainment, with no other effects
A completely unrelated area of life

quiz.q: How has technology changed the way citizens engage with government?
options: Citizens can no longer access any government information
It has made information about government actions more accessible
Technology has eliminated all forms of citizen participation
Technology has had no effect on citizen engagement

quiz.q: Which is an example of technology’s impact on communication with government?
options: Technology preventing all communication with government
Citizens accessing government services or information online
No connection between technology and government services
A complete absence of any online government services

quiz.q: Why might increased access to information through technology be significant for democracy?
options: Technology has no role in supporting democratic engagement
Access to information has no connection to democratic participation
Informed citizens can more effectively participate in democratic processes
Democracy functions identically with or without access to information

quiz.q: Why is it useful to study the relationship between technology and government?
options: Governance has remained completely unaffected by technology
Technology and government have no meaningful connection
It helps explain how modern tools are reshaping civic participation and governance
This relationship has no relevance to social studies

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing an Op-Ed (Opinion Editorial)
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Writing strand: an op-ed presents a writer’s opinion on a current issue, supported by reasoning and evidence, often published to persuade or inform public opinion.

quiz.q: An op-ed is written to ___.
options: Report only neutral facts with no opinion
Present the writer’s opinion on a current issue

quiz.q: A strong op-ed typically includes ___.
options: Only the writer’s name and nothing else
Reasoning and evidence to support the writer’s opinion

quiz.q: Why might a writer choose to write an op-ed about a current issue?
options: To persuade or inform public opinion on a topic they care about
Op-eds never discuss real or current issues
Op-eds are always purely factual with no opinion involved
Writing an op-ed serves no persuasive purpose

quiz.q: Which is an example of a strong op-ed opening?
options: This piece has no clear topic.
Our city urgently needs improved public transportation, and here is why.

quiz.q: Why is it useful for an op-ed to consider opposing viewpoints?
options: Considering other viewpoints always weakens an op-ed
This consideration has no effect on persuasive writing
Op-eds should never mention any opposing views
Addressing other perspectives can strengthen the credibility of the writer’s argument

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Probability: Expected Value
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Data Management strand: expected value is the average outcome you would predict over many trials, calculated by multiplying each possible outcome by its probability and adding the results together.

quiz.q: Expected value represents ___.
options: A value with no connection to probability
The average outcome predicted over many trials
A single guaranteed outcome every time

quiz.q: To calculate expected value, you ___.
options: Multiply each outcome by its probability and add the results
Only consider the highest possible outcome

quiz.q: If a game has a 50 percent chance of winning $10 and a 50 percent chance of winning $0, what is the expected value?
Why might a business use expected value when evaluating a decision involving risk?
options: Expected value guarantees an exact outcome every single time
Businesses never consider probability in decision-making
Expected value has no practical business application
It helps estimate the average outcome over many repeated situations

quiz.q: If a raffle ticket has a 1 in 100 chance of winning $200 and otherwise wins $0, what is its expected value?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Erosion, Deposition, and Landform Change
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: erosion moves weathered rock and soil, while deposition occurs when that material settles in a new location, together gradually reshaping landforms over time.

quiz.q: Erosion refers to the process of ___.
options: Moving weathered rock and soil to a new location

quiz.q: Deposition occurs when eroded material ___.
Which of these can cause both erosion and deposition?
A river delta is often formed through the process of ___.
options: Only erosion, with no deposition involved

quiz.q: Why might erosion and deposition together take thousands of years to significantly reshape a landform?
options: Landforms cannot change through natural processes
These processes have no connection to landform formation
Landform changes always occur within a single day
These processes typically occur gradually rather than instantly

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Climate Change Policy and International Agreements
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies People and Environments strand: countries often work together through international agreements to address climate change, setting shared goals for reducing greenhouse gas emissions and supporting sustainable development.

quiz.q: International agreements on climate change often aim to ___.
options: Prevent any country from taking environmental action
Set shared goals for reducing greenhouse gas emissions
Ignore the causes of climate change completely

quiz.q: Why might countries choose to cooperate through international climate agreements?
options: Climate change is a global issue requiring coordinated international action
Countries never need to work together on environmental policy
Climate change only affects a single country
International cooperation provides no benefit for environmental issues

quiz.q: Which is an example of a goal commonly included in climate policy agreements?
options: Reducing greenhouse gas emissions over time
Eliminating all forms of international cooperation

quiz.q: Sustainable development, often connected to climate policy, focuses on ___.
options: Ignoring the needs of future generations entirely
A concept unrelated to climate policy
Development that has no connection to environmental impact
Meeting current needs without compromising the ability of future generations to meet theirs

quiz.q: Why is it useful for students to study international climate policy?
options: Climate policy has no connection to global issues
International climate agreements have never actually existed
This topic has no relevance to social studies
It helps them understand how countries collaborate to address a shared global challenge

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Synthesizing Multiple Perspectives into a Cohesive Argument
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Reading strand: synthesizing multiple perspectives means combining ideas and evidence from several sources or viewpoints to form a well-supported, cohesive argument or understanding.

quiz.q: Synthesizing multiple perspectives means ___.
options: Copying one text exactly with no analysis
Combining ideas and evidence from several sources into a cohesive understanding
Reading only one source and ignoring all others

quiz.q: Why might a reader consult multiple perspectives on the same issue?
options: One perspective always provides a complete understanding
Additional perspectives never add useful information
To build a more complete and balanced understanding of the topic

quiz.q: When synthesizing perspectives, a reader should look for ___.
options: Connections, agreements, and disagreements across sources
Only identical information repeated in every source
Random, unrelated facts with no connections
Nothing related to the sources’ content

quiz.q: Which is an example of synthesizing multiple perspectives?
options: Copying a single paragraph exactly with no analysis
Reading only the title of one article
Combining ideas from several articles to form a well-rounded argument

quiz.q: Why is synthesizing considered an advanced reading and writing skill?
options: It has no connection to critical thinking
It only involves reading a single sentence
It requires understanding, comparing, and combining ideas from multiple sources into a new argument
It requires no understanding of any source

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Review: Algebra, Exponents, Probability, and Geometry
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Number and Geometry strands review: this lesson revisits systems of linear relations, exponent laws, expected value, angle relationships in polygons, and equations with variables on both sides.

quiz.q: Simplify: x squared times x to the fourth power.
Solve for x: 2x + 5 = x + 12.
What is the sum of interior angles in a hexagon (6 sides)?
If a raffle ticket has a 1 in 50 chance of winning $100, what is its expected value?
Why is it useful to review algebra, exponents, probability, and geometry together?
options: Review is never useful in math
Each topic must be learned in complete isolation
These topics have no connection to each other
These related math concepts reinforce each other for stronger overall understanding

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Review: Body Systems, Technology, and Earth Processes
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Science review: this lesson revisits the endocrine, respiratory, circulatory, and immune systems, robotics, WHMIS symbols, space exploration, and erosion and deposition covered across recent lessons.

quiz.q: Which body system regulates the body using hormones released into the bloodstream?
Which body system defends the body against harmful pathogens?
WHMIS symbols are used to communicate information about ___.
Erosion and deposition together gradually reshape ___.
Why is it valuable to review body systems, technology, and Earth processes together?
options: These topics are entirely unrelated to each other
Each topic must always be studied in isolation
It helps connect and reinforce related science concepts learned across recent lessons
Review provides no benefit in science

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Review: 20th-Century History and Global Citizenship
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 7 Social Studies review: this lesson revisits the Vietnam War, the fall of the Berlin Wall, apartheid, peacekeeping, multiculturalism, trade agreements, refugee crises, technology and government, and climate policy.

quiz.q: The fall of the Berlin Wall in 1989 symbolized the end of ___.
options: The founding of the United Nations

quiz.q: Apartheid was a system of racial segregation practiced in ___.
United Nations peacekeeping missions aim to ___.
options: Maintain or restore peace in regions affected by conflict

quiz.q: A refugee is generally someone forced to flee their home country due to ___.
Why is it useful to review 20th-century history and global citizenship topics together?
options: Each topic must be studied with no connection to the others
These topics have no meaningful connections
Review is never useful in social studies
It helps reinforce how historical events connect to ongoing global cooperation and citizenship today

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Close Reading Strategies for Complex Texts
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: close reading involves carefully rereading a text, annotating key details, and analyzing specific word choices to build a deeper understanding of challenging material.

quiz.q: Close reading involves ___.
options: Carefully rereading a text and analyzing specific details
Reading a text only once, as quickly as possible
Skipping difficult sections of a text entirely

quiz.q: Why might a reader annotate a text while close reading?
options: A reason unrelated to close reading
Annotating never helps a reader understand a text
To track important details, questions, and reactions as they read
Annotations always distract from understanding a text

quiz.q: Why is close reading especially useful for complex or challenging texts?
options: Close reading only works for simple, easy texts
Complex texts never benefit from careful, repeated reading
A reason unrelated to close reading
It helps break down difficult ideas into smaller, more manageable parts

quiz.q: Which is an example of a close reading strategy?
options: Rereading a confusing paragraph and noting unfamiliar vocabulary
Reading only the title of a text and nothing else
A concept unrelated to close reading
Ignoring any words that are difficult to understand

quiz.q: Why might a reader reread a text multiple times during close reading?
options: Each rereading can reveal new details or deepen understanding
A single reading always reveals everything about a text
Rereading a text is never useful for understanding it
A reason unrelated to close reading

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Solving and Graphing Inequalities on a Number Line
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: solving an inequality finds the range of values that make it true, and graphing it on a number line uses an open or closed circle and an arrow to show that range.

quiz.q: Solving an inequality finds ___.
options: No values at all that satisfy the statement
A single exact value that makes the statement true
A range of values that make the statement true

quiz.q: On a number line, an open circle at a point means ___.
options: A concept unrelated to graphing inequalities
The graph has no solution at all
That value is included in the solution
That value is not included in the solution

quiz.q: On a number line, a closed (filled-in) circle at a point means ___.
options: A concept unrelated to graphing inequalities
That value is included in the solution
That value is not included in the solution

quiz.q: If x ≥ 4, which type of circle would be used at 4 on the number line?
options: A circle unrelated to this inequality
No circle would be used at all

quiz.q: Solve for x: 2x < 10.
options: A solution unrelated to the inequality

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Newton’s Laws of Motion (Intro)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: Newton’s three laws of motion describe how objects behave when forces act on them, including inertia, the relationship between force and acceleration, and action-reaction pairs.

quiz.q: Newton’s first law of motion describes ___.
options: The speed of light in a vacuum
The relationship between force and colour
Inertia -- an object’s tendency to resist changes in motion
A concept unrelated to Newton’s laws

quiz.q: Newton’s second law of motion relates ___.
options: Only the temperature of an object
A concept unrelated to Newton’s laws
The volume and density of an object

quiz.q: Newton’s third law of motion states that ___.
options: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction
Objects in motion always eventually speed up on their own
Forces never have any reaction at all
A concept unrelated to Newton’s laws

quiz.q: Which is an example of Newton’s third law in action?
options: A ball sitting still on a table with no forces acting on it
A swimmer pushing water backward to move forward
A concept unrelated to Newton’s laws
An object that never interacts with any other object

quiz.q: Why are Newton’s laws considered fundamental to understanding motion?
options: Newton’s laws only apply to objects in outer space
These laws have no connection to how objects move
They explain how and why objects move or stay still under different forces

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The League of Nations and Its Legacy
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the League of Nations was an international organization formed after World War I to promote peace and cooperation between countries, though it ultimately struggled to prevent future conflict.

quiz.q: The League of Nations was formed after ___.
options: A conflict unrelated to the League of Nations

quiz.q: The main goal of the League of Nations was to ___.
options: Promote peace and cooperation between countries
Encourage new wars between member countries
A concept unrelated to the League of Nations

quiz.q: Which of these contributed to the League of Nations’ struggles?
options: It lacked the power to enforce its decisions on member countries
A reason unrelated to the League of Nations
It had no connection to preventing conflict
Every country in the world immediately joined and fully supported it

quiz.q: Why is the League of Nations often discussed alongside the later United Nations?
options: The United Nations was formed long before the League of Nations
A reason unrelated to international organizations
The League’s shortcomings influenced the design of the UN as its successor organization
The League of Nations and the United Nations have no historical connection

quiz.q: Why do historians study the League of Nations today?
options: The League of Nations has no relevance to understanding global history
It successfully prevented all future global conflicts
A reason unrelated to social studies learning
It offers important lessons about the challenges of international cooperation

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Parallel Structure in Complex Sentences
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: parallel structure in complex sentences means keeping grammatically similar elements consistent, even across clauses joined by conjunctions like “although” or “because.”

quiz.q: Parallel structure means keeping grammatically similar elements ___.
options: Randomly varied with no consistent pattern
Different from one another throughout a sentence

quiz.q: Which sentence uses correct parallel structure?
options: She enjoys hiking, biking, and swimming.
She enjoys hiking, biking, and to swim.
She enjoys hiking, to bike, and swimming.
A sentence unrelated to parallel structure

quiz.q: Which sentence uses correct parallel structure in a complex sentence?
options: Although he was tired, he finished his homework and cleaned his room.
Although he was tired, he finished his homework and was cleaning his room.
A sentence unrelated to parallel structure
Although being tired, he finished his homework and cleaned his room.

quiz.q: Why is parallel structure especially important in complex sentences with multiple clauses?
options: Complex sentences never need to follow consistent grammatical patterns
It keeps the sentence clear and grammatically consistent despite its added complexity
Parallel structure only matters in short, simple sentences

quiz.q: Which phrase breaks parallel structure?
options: A phrase unrelated to parallel structure
“to run, to jump, and to climb”

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Polynomials: Introduction to Terms and Like Terms
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: a polynomial is an expression made up of terms combined with addition or subtraction, and like terms have the same variable raised to the same power and can be combined.

quiz.q: A polynomial is an expression made up of ___.
options: Only a single number with no variables
Terms combined using addition or subtraction
Terms that can never be combined in any way

quiz.q: Like terms have ___.
options: Completely different variables from one another
No connection to variables at all
The same variable raised to the same power
A concept unrelated to like terms

quiz.q: Which of these are like terms?
Simplify: 4x + 3x.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why can 5y and 5y² not be combined as like terms?
options: A reason unrelated to like terms
They have the same variable but different exponents
Terms can never be combined under any circumstances

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Water Cycle and Its Connection to Climate
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: the water cycle describes how water moves through evaporation, condensation, and precipitation, and it plays a key role in shaping regional and global climate patterns.

quiz.q: The water cycle includes the processes of ___.
options: Only freezing, with no other processes involved
A single process that never repeats
A concept unrelated to the water cycle

quiz.q: Evaporation occurs when ___.
options: Water changes directly into a solid
Water changes from a liquid into a gas
Water changes from a gas into a liquid

quiz.q: Condensation occurs when ___.
options: Water vapour changes into liquid water droplets
Liquid water changes into water vapour

quiz.q: How does the water cycle influence regional climate?
options: Climate is determined with no involvement from water at all
A reason unrelated to the water cycle
It affects patterns of rainfall, humidity, and temperature in different regions
The water cycle has no connection to climate at all

quiz.q: Why might scientists study changes in the water cycle when studying climate change?
options: The water cycle has no connection to climate change
A reason unrelated to environmental science
Shifts in evaporation and precipitation patterns can signal broader climate changes
The water cycle never changes over time

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Immigration Policy: Historical Changes
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: Canada’s immigration policy has changed significantly over time, shifting from restrictive and discriminatory practices to a points-based system aimed at welcoming diverse newcomers.

quiz.q: Canada’s early immigration policies were often ___.
options: Focused only on welcoming immigrants from every country equally
Restrictive and discriminatory toward certain groups
Completely open to every immigrant with no restrictions at all
A concept unrelated to Canada’s immigration history

quiz.q: Canada’s modern immigration system often uses ___.
options: A concept unrelated to immigration policy
A system that only accepts immigrants from a single country
A points-based system considering factors like skills and education
A system that considers no criteria at all

quiz.q: Why might a country change its immigration policies over time?
options: Economic needs, social values, and global events can all influence policy changes
A reason unrelated to immigration policy
Immigration policies never change once they are established
Policy changes have no connection to a country’s needs or values

quiz.q: Why is it important to study the history of Canada’s immigration policy?
options: It helps explain how Canada’s population and society have developed and changed
Immigration policy has remained exactly the same throughout Canadian history
This history has no connection to understanding Canada today
A reason unrelated to social studies learning

quiz.q: Which of these reflects a shift toward more inclusive immigration policy?
options: Closing Canada’s borders to all immigration entirely
Adding new restrictions based on a person’s country of origin
A concept unrelated to immigration policy
Removing restrictions based on a person’s country of origin

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a Compare and Contrast Essay
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: a compare and contrast essay examines the similarities and differences between two subjects, often organized point-by-point or subject-by-subject.

quiz.q: A compare and contrast essay examines ___.
options: Only the differences, never any similarities
A single subject with no comparison at all
The similarities and differences between two subjects

quiz.q: In a point-by-point structure, a writer organizes the essay by ___.
options: Randomly mixing ideas with no clear organization
Discussing one point of comparison at a time for both subjects
A concept unrelated to essay structure
Discussing all of one subject, then all of the other subject

quiz.q: In a subject-by-subject structure, a writer organizes the essay by ___.
options: Discussing one point of comparison at a time for both subjects
Discussing all of one subject, then all of the other subject
A concept unrelated to essay structure

quiz.q: Which transition word might signal a similarity in a compare and contrast essay?
options: A word unrelated to signaling similarity

quiz.q: Why might a writer choose to compare and contrast two subjects in an essay?
options: To avoid discussing either subject in any detail
To help readers understand each subject more clearly through comparison
Comparing subjects never adds any value to an essay

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: The Pythagorean Theorem: Applications
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: the Pythagorean theorem (a² + b² = c²) can be applied to real-world problems, such as finding the shortest distance across a rectangular field or checking whether a corner is a right angle.

quiz.q: The Pythagorean theorem states that ___.
options: A formula unrelated to the Pythagorean theorem
a + b = c for any triangle
a² - b² = c² for any triangle
a² + b² = c², where c is the hypotenuse of a right triangle

quiz.q: If a right triangle has legs of 3 and 4, what is the length of the hypotenuse?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: If a right triangle has legs of 6 and 8, what is the length of the hypotenuse?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Which of these is a real-world application of the Pythagorean theorem?
options: Finding the average of a set of numbers
Finding the shortest diagonal distance across a rectangular field
Measuring the temperature of a room
A concept unrelated to the Pythagorean theorem

quiz.q: The Pythagorean theorem can only be applied to a triangle that is ___.
options: Any triangle with no specific requirements
A type of triangle unrelated to this theorem

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Sound Waves: Pitch, Volume, and Frequency
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: sound travels as waves, and its pitch is determined by frequency (how many vibrations per second) while its volume is determined by amplitude (the size of the vibration).

quiz.q: Pitch is determined by ___.
options: The colour of the sound wave
Frequency, or how many vibrations occur per second
The distance the sound has travelled

quiz.q: Volume is determined by ___.
options: Frequency, or how many vibrations occur per second
The temperature of the surrounding air
Amplitude, or the size of the vibration

quiz.q: A higher frequency sound wave produces a ___ pitch.
A larger amplitude sound wave produces a ___ volume.
Why might a small object, like a whistle, tend to produce a higher pitch than a large object, like a tuba?
options: Larger objects always produce higher pitches than smaller ones
A reason unrelated to sound waves
Object size has no connection to the pitch it produces
Smaller objects generally vibrate at higher frequencies than larger objects

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Marshall Plan and European Reconstruction
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the Marshall Plan was a large-scale American aid program launched after World War II to help rebuild the economies of war-torn European countries.

quiz.q: The Marshall Plan was launched to help ___.
options: Rebuild the economies of war-torn European countries after World War II
A concept unrelated to the Marshall Plan
Start a new conflict in Europe
End all trade between European nations

quiz.q: The Marshall Plan primarily provided European countries with ___.
options: Military weapons only, with no economic support
A type of support unrelated to the Marshall Plan

quiz.q: Why might economic aid have been especially important for Europe after World War II?
options: Many countries faced widespread destruction and needed support to rebuild
European countries faced no economic challenges after the war
Economic aid had no effect on rebuilding efforts
A reason unrelated to post-war reconstruction

quiz.q: Which country launched the Marshall Plan?
options: A country that no longer exists today
A country unrelated to the Marshall Plan

quiz.q: Why is the Marshall Plan still studied as an example of international cooperation?
options: It shows how aid can help rebuild economies and stability after a major conflict
A reason unrelated to its historical significance
It focused only on military strategy, with no economic component
The Marshall Plan had no lasting effect on Europe’s recovery

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Analogies and Word Relationships
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: an analogy compares two pairs of words that share a similar relationship, such as synonym, antonym, cause-and-effect, or part-to-whole relationships.

quiz.q: An analogy compares two pairs of words that share a ___.
Complete the analogy: Petal is to flower as page is to ___.
options: A word unrelated to the analogy

quiz.q: What relationship does the analogy “spark is to fire as seed is to plant” show?
Complete the analogy: Enormous is to huge as tiny is to ___.
options: A word unrelated to the analogy

quiz.q: Why are analogies a useful vocabulary tool?
options: Analogies only apply to numbers, not words
Analogies never help with understanding vocabulary
A concept unrelated to vocabulary building
They help build understanding of relationships between words and concepts

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Volume and Surface Area of Spheres (Intro)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: the volume of a sphere is found using the formula (4/3)πr³, and its surface area is found using the formula 4πr², where r is the radius.

quiz.q: The formula for the volume of a sphere is ___.
options: A formula unrelated to the volume of a sphere

quiz.q: The formula for the surface area of a sphere is ___.
options: A formula unrelated to the surface area of a sphere

quiz.q: In the sphere formulas, what does “r” represent?
If a sphere has a radius of 3, what is its approximate volume using π ≈ 3.14?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why might understanding the volume of a sphere be useful in real life?
options: Sphere volume calculations have no real-world use
Spheres never have a measurable volume
It helps calculate the capacity of round objects, like balls or tanks

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Structures: Load, Stress, and Material Strength
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: a structure must withstand different types of load and stress, and engineers select materials based on their strength, flexibility, and ability to resist these forces.

quiz.q: Load refers to ___.
options: The colour of a structure’s materials
The height of a structure alone
The weight or force a structure must support

quiz.q: Stress in a structure refers to ___.
options: A structure with no forces acting on it at all
The internal force experienced by a material under load
The external appearance of a structure

quiz.q: Why might an engineer choose a flexible material for a structure in an earthquake-prone area?
options: A reason unrelated to materials engineering
Flexibility has no connection to how a structure performs in an earthquake
Rigid materials are always safer in earthquake-prone areas
Flexible materials can absorb and withstand shifting forces without breaking

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a structure experiencing load?
options: A bridge supporting the weight of passing vehicles
A material with no connection to forces at all
A structure with nothing resting on or pushing against it

quiz.q: Why do engineers test the strength of materials before building a structure?
options: Testing materials provides no useful information for construction
To ensure the materials can safely support the expected loads and stresses
Materials never need to be tested before use

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Decolonization in Africa: An Overview
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: decolonization in Africa refers to the process, largely occurring in the mid-20th century, by which African nations gained independence from European colonial powers.

quiz.q: Decolonization in Africa refers to the process by which ___.
options: African nations gained independence from European colonial powers
European powers expanded their control over African nations
African nations became colonies for the first time

quiz.q: Decolonization in Africa largely occurred during ___.
options: Ancient times, thousands of years ago
A time period unrelated to decolonization in Africa

quiz.q: Which of these was a common challenge many newly independent African nations faced?
options: Having no challenges at all after gaining independence
Building new governments and economies after colonial rule

quiz.q: Why is the history of decolonization in Africa significant to study?
options: Decolonization in Africa never actually took place
It helps explain the political and economic landscape of many African nations today
A reason unrelated to social studies learning
This history has no connection to understanding the world today

quiz.q: Which of these best describes the goal of independence movements in Africa?
options: Preventing any future political change in the region
Strengthening colonial control over African nations
Achieving self-governance and freedom from colonial rule
A concept unrelated to independence movements

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Flashback and Foreshadowing Together
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: flashback interrupts a story to show a past event, while foreshadowing hints at a future event, and authors often use both techniques together to build a richer narrative.

quiz.q: A flashback interrupts a story to show ___.
options: A concept unrelated to narrative techniques
An event happening in the present moment only

quiz.q: Foreshadowing hints at ___.
options: A future event in the story
A concept unrelated to narrative techniques
An event that has no connection to the plot
A past event in the story

quiz.q: Why might an author use both flashback and foreshadowing in the same story?
options: To build a richer narrative that connects past, present, and future events
These two techniques can never be used together
A reason unrelated to narrative structure
To confuse the reader with no clear purpose

quiz.q: Which is an example of a flashback?
options: A character suddenly remembering an event from years earlier
A character noticing dark clouds before a coming storm
A character describing their current surroundings

quiz.q: How might recognizing flashback and foreshadowing improve a reader’s understanding of a story?
options: Recognizing these techniques makes a story harder to understand
These techniques never affect how a reader understands a story
It helps reveal how the past, present, and future connect within the narrative
A reason unrelated to reading comprehension

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Ratio and Rate Problems with Multiple Steps
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: multi-step ratio and rate problems require combining several calculations, such as finding a unit rate and then using it to solve for a larger or smaller quantity.

quiz.q: A multi-step ratio problem often requires ___.
options: A concept unrelated to ratio problems
Only a single calculation with no additional steps
Combining several calculations to reach a final answer

quiz.q: If a car travels 150 km in 3 hours, what is its unit rate in km per hour?
options: A rate unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: If a recipe uses a ratio of 2 cups of flour for every 3 cups of sugar, how many cups of flour are needed for 9 cups of sugar?
options: An amount unrelated to the ratio

quiz.q: If 5 workers can build a fence in 10 hours, how many hours would it take 10 workers, assuming the same rate per worker?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why are multi-step ratio problems useful to practice?
options: They build skills for solving more complex, real-world proportional situations
A reason unrelated to ratio problems
They only apply to problems with a single possible step
Multi-step ratio problems have no practical use

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Digestive System in Depth
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: the digestive system breaks down food into nutrients the body can absorb and use, involving organs such as the stomach, small intestine, and large intestine.

quiz.q: The main function of the digestive system is to ___.
options: A function unrelated to the digestive system
Filter air before it reaches the lungs
Break down food into nutrients the body can absorb and use

quiz.q: Which organ is primarily responsible for breaking down food using acid and enzymes?
The small intestine plays a key role in ___.
options: A role unrelated to the small intestine
Pumping blood to the rest of the body

quiz.q: The large intestine mainly functions to ___.
options: Break food down using stomach acid
A function unrelated to the large intestine
Absorb water and form waste for elimination

quiz.q: Why is the digestive system considered essential for overall health?
options: The digestive system has no connection to the body’s overall health
It provides the body with the nutrients and energy needed to function
The body can function normally with no digestive system at all

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Non-Aligned Movement During the Cold War
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the Non-Aligned Movement was a group of countries during the Cold War that chose not to formally align with either the United States or the Soviet Union.

quiz.q: The Non-Aligned Movement was made up of countries that chose not to ___.
options: Formally align with either the United States or the Soviet Union
Communicate with any other countries during the Cold War
A concept unrelated to the Non-Aligned Movement
Participate in any international organizations at all

quiz.q: Why might a country have chosen to join the Non-Aligned Movement?
options: To maintain independence from the two major Cold War powers
To become fully controlled by one of the two major powers
Joining provided no benefit to member countries at all
A reason unrelated to the Non-Aligned Movement

quiz.q: The Cold War was primarily a period of tension between ___.
options: Every country in the world equally
The United States and the Soviet Union
Two countries unrelated to the Cold War

quiz.q: Why is the Non-Aligned Movement an important part of Cold War history?
options: It shows that many countries sought an independent path rather than choosing a side
A reason unrelated to Cold War history
It shows that every country in the world was forced to pick a side
The Non-Aligned Movement had no real impact on world history

quiz.q: Which of these best describes the overall goal of the Non-Aligned Movement?
options: Ending all international cooperation between countries
Pursuing an independent path in international relations
A concept unrelated to the Non-Aligned Movement
Strengthening one side of the Cold War over the other

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Evaluating Online Sources for Credibility
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: evaluating an online source for credibility involves checking its author, publication date, purpose, and whether its claims are supported by reliable evidence.

quiz.q: Evaluating an online source for credibility involves checking its ___.
options: A concept unrelated to media literacy
Author, publication date, purpose, and supporting evidence
Font style and background colour only
Number of images used on the page

quiz.q: Why is it important to check who authored an online source?
options: A reason unrelated to evaluating sources
Every author is equally credible regardless of their background
The author of a source never affects its credibility
It helps determine whether the author has relevant expertise or potential bias

quiz.q: Why might checking the publication date of a source matter?
options: A reason unrelated to evaluating sources
It helps determine whether the information is current and still accurate
Older sources are always more reliable than newer ones
The publication date never affects how reliable a source is

quiz.q: Which is a warning sign that an online source may not be credible?
options: It clearly cites its sources and evidence
It makes claims with no supporting evidence or citations
It is written by an expert in the field
A concept unrelated to evaluating sources

quiz.q: Why is evaluating source credibility an important skill today?
options: All online information can be trusted without question
A reason unrelated to media literacy
This skill has no use when reading information online
It helps readers avoid being misled by inaccurate or biased information online

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Probability: Complementary Events
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: complementary events are two outcomes where exactly one must occur, and their probabilities always add up to 1 (or 100%).

quiz.q: Complementary events are two outcomes where ___.
options: Exactly one of the two must occur
A concept unrelated to complementary events
Both outcomes must occur at the same time

quiz.q: The probabilities of two complementary events always add up to ___.
options: A value unrelated to complementary events

quiz.q: If the probability of rain tomorrow is 30%, what is the probability it will not rain?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: If the probability of drawing a red marble from a bag is 2/5, what is the probability of not drawing a red marble?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why are complementary events useful in probability?
options: Complementary events have no practical use in probability
They allow one probability to be calculated easily if the other is already known
They can only be used when there are more than two possible outcomes
A reason unrelated to complementary events

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Fossil Formation and the Geologic Time Scale
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: fossils form when the remains or traces of living things are preserved in rock over long periods of time, and the geologic time scale organizes Earth’s history into different eras and periods.

quiz.q: Fossils typically form when ___.
options: A concept unrelated to fossil formation
Rocks form with no connection to living things at all
The remains or traces of living things are preserved in rock over time
A living thing is preserved instantly with no time required

quiz.q: The geologic time scale organizes Earth’s history into ___.
options: Only the years since human history began
A single unbroken block of time
A concept unrelated to the geologic time scale

quiz.q: Why are fossils useful to scientists studying Earth’s history?
options: Fossils provide no useful scientific information
They provide evidence about past life forms and environmental conditions
Fossils only reveal information about modern living things

quiz.q: Which of these is required for a fossil to form?
options: The complete absence of any rock or sediment
A living thing that has never existed
Conditions that allow remains to be preserved rather than decompose completely
A concept unrelated to fossil formation

quiz.q: Why might scientists use the geologic time scale when studying fossils?
options: Fossils can never be placed within a specific time period
The geologic time scale has no connection to studying fossils
A reason unrelated to Earth science
It helps place fossils within a specific era, giving context to when a species existed

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s National Parks and Environmental Policy
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: Canada’s national parks protect significant natural areas, and environmental policy shapes how the country balances conservation with development and resource use.

quiz.q: Canada’s national parks are primarily established to ___.
options: Protect significant natural areas and biodiversity
A concept unrelated to national parks
Encourage unrestricted development in natural areas
Remove all public access to natural areas

quiz.q: Environmental policy generally aims to balance ___.
options: A concept unrelated to environmental policy
Conservation with development and resource use
Conservation alone, with no consideration for the economy
Development alone, with no consideration for the environment

quiz.q: Why might governments create environmental policies related to national parks?
options: A reason unrelated to environmental policy
National parks require no policies or protections at all
Environmental policies have no connection to protecting natural areas
To help protect natural areas for future generations while managing human impact

quiz.q: Which of these might be a challenge in balancing conservation and development?
options: A concept unrelated to environmental policy
Weighing the economic benefits of development against environmental protection
Conservation and development always align perfectly with no tension
There are never any challenges involved in this balance

quiz.q: Why is it valuable for students to learn about environmental policy?
options: A reason unrelated to social studies learning
Environmental policy is decided with no input from society at all
It helps them understand how societies make decisions about protecting natural resources
Environmental policy has no connection to understanding society

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a Process Analysis Essay (How Something Works)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: a process analysis essay explains how something works or how to complete a task, breaking the process down into clear, logically ordered steps.

quiz.q: A process analysis essay explains ___.
options: How something works or how to complete a task
A fictional story with no factual basis

quiz.q: A process analysis essay should break its topic down into ___.
options: A single confusing paragraph with no structure
A concept unrelated to process analysis essays

quiz.q: Which of these would be an appropriate topic for a process analysis essay?
options: A persuasive argument about a controversial topic
How a bill becomes a law
A concept unrelated to process analysis essays

quiz.q: Why might a writer use transition words like “first,” “next,” and “finally” in a process analysis essay?
options: They are only used in fictional stories
A reason unrelated to process analysis essays
They help guide the reader clearly through each step of the process
These words are never useful in this type of essay

quiz.q: Why is it important for a process analysis essay to be written in a logical order?
options: Steps presented out of order can confuse the reader and make the process hard to follow
A process analysis essay does not need any clear order at all
The order of steps never affects how clearly a process is understood

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Standard Form vs. Expanded Form of Numbers
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: standard form writes a number using digits, such as 4,502, while expanded form breaks it down by place value, such as 4,000 + 500 + 2.

quiz.q: Standard form writes a number using ___.
options: A concept unrelated to standard form
Digits, in its usual numerical form
Only words, with no digits at all

quiz.q: Expanded form breaks a number down by ___.
options: A concept unrelated to expanded form
Its total value with no breakdown at all

quiz.q: What is 3,406 written in expanded form?
options: A value unrelated to the conversion

quiz.q: What is 2,000 + 300 + 70 + 5 written in standard form?
options: A value unrelated to the conversion

quiz.q: Why might expanded form be useful when teaching place value?
options: Expanded form is only used with very small numbers
Expanded form has no connection to understanding place value
A reason unrelated to expanded form
It clearly shows the value each digit represents based on its position

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Symbiosis: Mutualism, Commensalism, and Parasitism
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: symbiosis describes close relationships between different species, including mutualism (both benefit), commensalism (one benefits, the other is unaffected), and parasitism (one benefits at the other’s expense).

quiz.q: In a mutualistic relationship, ___.
options: Only one species benefits, while the other is harmed

quiz.q: In a commensal relationship, ___.
options: One species benefits while the other is unaffected

quiz.q: In a parasitic relationship, ___.
options: Neither species is affected in any way
One species benefits at the expense of the other

quiz.q: Which is an example of a mutualistic relationship?
options: Two species that never interact with one another
A tick feeding on a dog’s blood, harming the dog
Bees pollinating flowers while feeding on their nectar

quiz.q: Why do scientists study symbiotic relationships in ecosystems?
options: Symbiotic relationships provide no useful information about ecosystems
They help explain how different species depend on and affect one another
Species in an ecosystem never interact with each other

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Arab Spring and Its Global Impact
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the Arab Spring was a wave of pro-democracy protests and uprisings that spread across several Middle Eastern and North African countries beginning in 2010 and 2011.

quiz.q: The Arab Spring was a wave of ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the Arab Spring

quiz.q: The Arab Spring began spreading across the Middle East and North Africa around ___.
options: A time period unrelated to the Arab Spring
Ancient times, thousands of years ago

quiz.q: Which of these was a common demand of protesters during the Arab Spring?
options: A demand unrelated to the Arab Spring
A return to stricter authoritarian governments
Greater political freedom and an end to authoritarian rule
The complete removal of all elections

quiz.q: Why did the Arab Spring have such a widespread impact across the region?
options: The events in one country had no effect on any other country
The protests were limited to a single city with no broader impact
A reason unrelated to the Arab Spring
Similar political and economic frustrations existed in multiple countries at the same time

quiz.q: Why do historians and social studies students continue to study the Arab Spring?
options: A reason unrelated to its historical significance
The events of the Arab Spring have been completely forgotten
It offers insight into how citizen movements can influence political change
The Arab Spring had no lasting effect on the region

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Using Semicolons, Colons, and Dashes
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: semicolons join related independent clauses, colons introduce lists or explanations, and dashes can be used to add emphasis or set off extra information.

quiz.q: A semicolon can be used to ___.
options: Replace every comma in a sentence
End a sentence in place of a period
Join two closely related independent clauses

quiz.q: A colon is often used to ___.
options: Separate every word in a sentence

quiz.q: A dash can be used to ___.
options: Indicate the end of a paragraph
Add emphasis or set off extra information
Replace all punctuation in a sentence

quiz.q: Which sentence correctly uses a colon?
options: A sentence unrelated to colon use
She packed three things: a map, water, and snacks.
She packed three things; a map, water, and snacks.
She packed three things, a map, water, and snacks.

quiz.q: Why might a writer choose a dash instead of a comma to set off extra information?
options: A dash can create a stronger emphasis or a more dramatic pause
Dashes always weaken the emphasis of a sentence
Dashes are never used to set off extra information

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Box-and-Whisker Plots (Intro)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: a box-and-whisker plot displays a data set’s minimum, first quartile, median, third quartile, and maximum, helping to visualize its spread and central tendency.

quiz.q: A box-and-whisker plot displays a data set’s ___.
options: Only the average of the data set
Only the maximum value in the data set
A concept unrelated to box-and-whisker plots

quiz.q: The line inside the box of a box-and-whisker plot typically represents the ___.
options: A value unrelated to box-and-whisker plots

quiz.q: The “whiskers” of a box-and-whisker plot extend to show the ___.
options: Minimum and maximum values in the data set
Only the values within the box itself
A concept unrelated to box-and-whisker plots

quiz.q: Why might a box-and-whisker plot be useful for comparing two data sets?
options: A reason unrelated to box-and-whisker plots
It visually shows the spread and central tendency of each set side by side
Box-and-whisker plots cannot be used to compare data sets
They only display a single number with no visual comparison

quiz.q: What does the “box” portion of a box-and-whisker plot represent?
options: The entire range of the data set
Only the single highest value in the data set
A concept unrelated to box-and-whisker plots
The middle 50% of the data, between the first and third quartiles

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Nuclear Energy: Fission and Power Generation
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: nuclear energy is produced through fission, a process where atomic nuclei split apart and release large amounts of energy, which can be used to generate electricity.

quiz.q: Nuclear fission occurs when ___.
options: An atom disappears with no reaction at all
Two atomic nuclei combine into one
An atomic nucleus splits apart, releasing energy
A process unrelated to nuclear fission

quiz.q: Nuclear power plants use fission to ___.
options: Generate heat that is used to produce electricity
A process unrelated to nuclear power
Cool down surrounding areas with no energy production
Directly power vehicles with no electricity involved

quiz.q: Which of these is a potential benefit of nuclear energy?
options: It requires no safety considerations of any kind
It can generate large amounts of electricity without directly burning fossil fuels
A concept unrelated to nuclear energy
It produces no usable energy at all

quiz.q: Which of these is a potential concern associated with nuclear energy?
options: A concept unrelated to nuclear energy
It cannot generate any electricity at all
Nuclear energy has no potential concerns of any kind

quiz.q: Why might countries consider nuclear energy as part of their energy strategy?
options: It has no connection to generating electricity
Nuclear energy provides no benefits to a country’s energy strategy
A reason unrelated to nuclear energy
It can provide a significant, relatively low-carbon source of electricity

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Urban Sprawl and City Planning Challenges
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: urban sprawl refers to the rapid, spread-out expansion of a city into surrounding areas, creating challenges for transportation, infrastructure, and city planning.

quiz.q: Urban sprawl refers to ___.
options: A city shrinking in population and size
The rapid, spread-out expansion of a city into surrounding areas
A concept unrelated to urban sprawl
A city that never grows or changes over time

quiz.q: Which of these is a common challenge associated with urban sprawl?
options: A significant reduction in the need for transportation
A concept unrelated to urban sprawl
Increased dependence on cars and longer commute times
A city with no transportation challenges at all

quiz.q: Why might urban sprawl create challenges for city infrastructure?
options: Infrastructure needs are never affected by how a city is developed
Spread-out development can be more costly and difficult to service with roads, water, and utilities
Spread-out cities always require less infrastructure than compact ones
A reason unrelated to urban sprawl

quiz.q: Which of these is a strategy city planners might use to address urban sprawl?
options: Encouraging development to spread out as much as possible
Avoiding any planning or strategy at all
A concept unrelated to city planning

quiz.q: Why is urban sprawl an important topic in city planning?
options: Urban sprawl is not a real or relevant issue for city planners
Urban sprawl has no effect on how a city functions
It affects transportation, the environment, and quality of life in a growing city
A reason unrelated to city planning

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Nonfiction Text Structures
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: nonfiction texts are often organized using structures like cause-and-effect, problem-and-solution, or sequence, and recognizing these structures helps readers follow the author’s ideas.

quiz.q: Nonfiction texts are often organized using structures such as ___.
options: A concept unrelated to nonfiction text structures
A single unchanging structure used in every text

quiz.q: A cause-and-effect structure explains ___.
options: How one event leads to another
Events with no connection to one another
The steps needed to solve a problem

quiz.q: A problem-and-solution structure presents ___.
options: Only a problem, with no solution ever discussed
A problem, followed by one or more possible solutions
A solution with no explanation of the original problem

quiz.q: A sequence structure organizes information ___.
options: In a completely random order every time
With no order or organization at all
In a specific order, such as chronological order

quiz.q: Why is it useful for readers to recognize a text’s structure?
options: A reason unrelated to reading comprehension
It helps them anticipate how information will be organized and better follow the author’s ideas
All nonfiction texts use exactly the same structure
Recognizing text structure never helps with understanding a text

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Adding and Subtracting Polynomials
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: adding or subtracting polynomials involves combining like terms while keeping unlike terms separate.

quiz.q: To add polynomials, you ___.
options: A method unrelated to adding polynomials

quiz.q: Simplify: (3x + 5) + (2x + 1).
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Simplify: (7x + 4) - (2x + 1).
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: When subtracting polynomials, what must be done to the terms in the second polynomial?
options: They must be multiplied by 2 before combining
Their signs are never changed during subtraction
A step unrelated to subtracting polynomials
Their signs must be changed before combining like terms

quiz.q: Why must like terms be combined carefully when adding or subtracting polynomials?
options: Polynomials never require any careful combination of terms
Only terms with the same variable and exponent can be combined accurately
Any two terms can always be combined regardless of their variable or exponent

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Skeletal and Muscular Systems in Depth
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: the skeletal system provides structure, protection, and support for the body, while the muscular system works with it through contraction and relaxation to produce movement.

quiz.q: The skeletal system provides the body with ___.
options: A function unrelated to the skeletal system

quiz.q: Muscles produce movement through the process of ___.
Which is an example of the skeletal and muscular systems working together?
options: A concept unrelated to these systems
Breathing using only the lungs, with no involvement from bones or muscles
Bending your arm at the elbow using both bone and muscle
Digesting a meal using only the stomach

quiz.q: Why might bones be described as providing a framework for the body?
options: The body has no structure or framework at all
Bones have no connection to the body’s overall shape
A reason unrelated to the skeletal system
They give the body its overall shape and structure, supporting other systems

quiz.q: Why do the skeletal and muscular systems need to work closely together?
options: A reason unrelated to body systems
Movement occurs with no involvement from bones or muscles
Muscles need bones to pull against in order to create controlled movement
These two systems function completely independently of one another

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Global Food Security and Agriculture
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: food security means having reliable access to enough safe, nutritious food, and global agriculture faces challenges such as climate change, population growth, and unequal distribution of resources.

quiz.q: Food security refers to ___.
options: A concern that only applies to a single country
Having access to an unlimited supply of any type of food
Having reliable access to enough safe, nutritious food
A concept unrelated to food security

quiz.q: Which of these is a challenge facing global agriculture today?
options: A complete absence of any global food challenges
A concept unrelated to agricultural challenges
Climate change affecting crop yields and growing conditions
An unlimited and endlessly reliable food supply everywhere

quiz.q: Why might population growth create challenges for food security?
options: A growing population requires more food production and resources to meet demand
Population growth has no connection to food security
A larger population automatically guarantees enough food for everyone
A reason unrelated to food security

quiz.q: Which of these could help improve food security in a region?
options: A concept unrelated to food security
Reducing food production without any other changes
Investing in sustainable farming techniques and infrastructure

quiz.q: Why is global food security an important topic in social studies?
options: Food security has no connection to social studies topics
Food security is a concern only in fictional scenarios
A reason unrelated to this unit
It connects to economics, geography, and the well-being of people around the world

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a Eulogy or Tribute Speech
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: a eulogy or tribute speech honours a person by sharing meaningful memories, qualities, and the impact they had on others, often organized around specific stories or themes.

quiz.q: A eulogy or tribute speech is written to ___.
options: A concept unrelated to this type of writing
Criticize a person’s actions and character
Honour a person by sharing meaningful memories and qualities
Provide only a list of dates and facts with no personal reflection

quiz.q: Which of these might a tribute speech include?
options: A list of unrelated statistics with no connection to the person
Criticism of the person being honoured
A concept unrelated to tribute speeches
A specific story that reflects the person’s character

quiz.q: Why might a speaker organize a tribute speech around specific themes, such as kindness or determination?
options: It helps highlight meaningful qualities in a clear, memorable way
A reason unrelated to tribute speeches
Themes make a speech harder for an audience to follow
Themes are never used to organize this type of speech

quiz.q: Why is tone especially important in a eulogy or tribute speech?
options: A respectful, sincere tone helps honour the person appropriately
A reason unrelated to this type of writing
A humorous, disrespectful tone is always most appropriate
Tone has no effect on how a tribute speech is received

quiz.q: Which of these is a purpose of a tribute speech?
options: To provide a purely factual, emotionless account of events
A concept unrelated to tribute speeches
To focus only on negative memories of a person
To celebrate and remember the impact a person had on others

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Review: Polynomials, Pythagorean Applications, and Data Displays
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 51-60, including polynomials, the Pythagorean theorem, spheres, and box-and-whisker plots.

quiz.q: A polynomial is an expression made up of ___.
options: Terms combined using addition or subtraction
Terms that can never be combined in any way
Only a single number with no variables

quiz.q: The Pythagorean theorem states that ___.
options: a² + b² = c², where c is the hypotenuse of a right triangle
a² - b² = c² for any triangle
A formula unrelated to the Pythagorean theorem
a + b = c for any triangle

quiz.q: The formula for the volume of a sphere is ___.
options: A formula unrelated to the volume of a sphere

quiz.q: The line inside the box of a box-and-whisker plot typically represents the ___.
options: A value unrelated to box-and-whisker plots

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review polynomials, geometry, and data displays together?
options: These topics have no connection to each other
A reason unrelated to reviewing math
It reinforces how these math concepts connect and build on one another
Review never helps strengthen understanding of a subject

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Review: Motion, Sound, Structures, and Systems
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 51-60, including Newton’s laws, sound waves, structures, symbiosis, and the skeletal and muscular systems.

quiz.q: Newton’s first law of motion describes ___.
options: A concept unrelated to Newton’s laws
The relationship between force and colour
The speed of light in a vacuum
Inertia -- an object’s tendency to resist changes in motion

quiz.q: Pitch is determined by ___.
options: Frequency, or how many vibrations occur per second
The colour of the sound wave
The distance the sound has travelled

quiz.q: In a mutualistic relationship, ___.
options: Only one species benefits, while the other is harmed

quiz.q: Muscles produce movement through the process of ___.
Why is it useful to review motion, sound, structures, and body systems together?
options: Each topic must be studied with no connection to the others
It reinforces how these interconnected science concepts relate to one another
Review is never useful in science
These topics have no connection to each other

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Review: International Relations and Canadian Policy
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 51-60, including the League of Nations, Canadian immigration policy, decolonization, the Non-Aligned Movement, and the Arab Spring.

quiz.q: The League of Nations was formed after ___.
options: A conflict unrelated to the League of Nations

quiz.q: Canada’s modern immigration system often uses ___.
options: A system that only accepts immigrants from a single country
A concept unrelated to immigration policy
A points-based system considering factors like skills and education
A system that considers no criteria at all

quiz.q: Decolonization in Africa refers to the process by which ___.
options: African nations became colonies for the first time
European powers expanded their control over African nations
African nations gained independence from European colonial powers

quiz.q: The Arab Spring was a wave of ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the Arab Spring

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review international relations and policy topics together?
options: A reason unrelated to social studies learning
It reinforces how these interconnected historical and political events shaped the modern world
These topics have no connection to one another
Review is never useful when studying history and policy

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Author’s Purpose and Craft
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: analyzing an author’s purpose and craft means identifying why a writer chose specific words, structures, or techniques to inform, persuade, or entertain readers.

quiz.q: An author’s purpose for writing is often to ___.
options: Confuse readers with no clear goal
A concept unrelated to author’s purpose

quiz.q: Craft in writing refers to ___.
options: Only the length of a piece of writing
A concept unrelated to writing craft
Random word choices with no purpose
The deliberate techniques and choices a writer uses

quiz.q: Which is an example of analyzing an author’s craft?
options: A concept unrelated to analyzing craft
Only counting the number of paragraphs
Ignoring all of the author’s word choices
Noticing why a writer repeats a phrase for emphasis

quiz.q: If a text mainly explains how something works, its primary purpose is likely to ___.
options: A purpose unrelated to explanatory texts

quiz.q: Why is it useful for readers to consider an author’s purpose?
options: It helps readers understand why the text was written and how to interpret it
Every text has exactly the same purpose
A reason unrelated to reading comprehension
Purpose never affects how a text should be read

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Adding and Subtracting Fractions with Unlike Denominators
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: adding or subtracting fractions with unlike denominators requires finding a common denominator before combining the numerators.

quiz.q: To add or subtract fractions with unlike denominators, you must first ___.
options: A concept unrelated to fraction operations

quiz.q: What is 1/4 + 1/2?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: What is 2/3 - 1/6?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: What is the least common denominator of 1/3 and 1/4?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why is a common denominator needed when adding fractions?
options: It ensures the fractions represent equal-sized parts that can be combined accurately
Common denominators are never needed for this operation
A reason unrelated to fraction operations
Denominators never affect how fractions are added

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Nervous System: Brain, Spinal Cord, and Neurons
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: the nervous system, made up of the brain, spinal cord, and neurons, controls the body by sending and receiving electrical signals to coordinate actions and responses.

quiz.q: The nervous system is primarily made up of the brain, spinal cord, and ___.
options: A structure unrelated to the nervous system

quiz.q: The main function of the nervous system is to ___.
options: Send and receive signals to control the body’s actions and responses
A function unrelated to the nervous system

quiz.q: A neuron is a specialized cell that ___.
options: Transmits electrical signals throughout the body

quiz.q: Which part of the nervous system acts as the body’s main control centre?
options: An organ unrelated to the nervous system

quiz.q: Why is the spinal cord considered a critical part of the nervous system?
options: The spinal cord has no connection to sending signals
A reason unrelated to the nervous system
The spinal cord only affects digestion
It carries signals between the brain and the rest of the body

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Fur Trade and Its Impact on Indigenous Peoples and Settlers
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the fur trade was an early economic system in Canada built on the exchange of furs, which relied heavily on partnerships and knowledge shared between Indigenous peoples and European settlers.

quiz.q: The fur trade was primarily built on the exchange of ___.
options: Furs, especially beaver pelts, for goods
A concept unrelated to the fur trade

quiz.q: The fur trade relied heavily on partnerships between ___.
options: Groups with no connection to trade at all
Only European countries with each other
A concept unrelated to the fur trade

quiz.q: Which skill did Indigenous peoples often contribute to the success of the fur trade?
options: No useful skills or knowledge at all
A concept unrelated to the fur trade
Knowledge of the land and trapping techniques
Knowledge of overseas shipping routes only

quiz.q: Why did the fur trade have such a significant impact on early Canada?
options: The fur trade only affected European countries directly
The fur trade had no effect on Canada’s early development
It shaped economic relationships, exploration, and settlement patterns
A reason unrelated to the fur trade

quiz.q: Why do historians study the fur trade today?
options: It helps explain early relationships between Indigenous peoples and European settlers
The fur trade never actually took place in Canada
The fur trade has no historical significance
A reason unrelated to Canadian history

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a Business Letter of Complaint or Request
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: a business letter of complaint or request uses formal language and a clear structure to address an issue or ask for action from a company or organization.

quiz.q: A business letter of complaint should use ___.
options: No greeting or closing at all
A concept unrelated to business letters
