quiz.q: Which is an appropriate opening for a business letter?
options: A greeting unrelated to formal letters

quiz.q: A letter of complaint should clearly state ___.
options: The problem and what resolution is being requested
A concept unrelated to letters of complaint

quiz.q: Which closing is appropriate for a formal business letter?
options: A closing unrelated to formal letters

quiz.q: Why is a clear, specific description of the problem important in a complaint letter?
options: Vague complaints are always more effective
A reason unrelated to letter writing
Specific details only confuse the reader
It helps the reader understand exactly what needs to be fixed

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multiplying and Dividing Fractions and Mixed Numbers
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: multiplying fractions involves multiplying numerators and denominators directly, while dividing fractions involves multiplying by the reciprocal of the divisor.

quiz.q: To multiply two fractions, you ___.
options: Multiply the numerators together and the denominators together
A concept unrelated to multiplying fractions

quiz.q: What is 1/2 x 2/3?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: To divide by a fraction, you multiply by its ___.
options: A concept unrelated to dividing fractions

quiz.q: What is 3/4 ÷ 1/2?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: How do you convert a mixed number like 2 1/3 into an improper fraction?
options: Multiply the whole number by the denominator and add the numerator
A method unrelated to mixed numbers
Add the whole number and denominator together
Multiply the whole number by the numerator only

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Volcanoes and Earthquakes: Causes and Effects
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: volcanoes and earthquakes are often caused by the movement of tectonic plates, releasing built-up pressure and energy that can dramatically reshape the Earth’s surface.

quiz.q: Volcanoes and earthquakes are often caused by ___.
options: A concept unrelated to volcanoes and earthquakes
A sudden increase in ocean temperature

quiz.q: An earthquake occurs when ___.
options: Built-up pressure along a fault is suddenly released
The Earth’s surface remains perfectly still

quiz.q: A volcano erupts when ___.
options: A concept unrelated to volcanic eruptions
Magma and gases escape from beneath the Earth’s surface
The Earth’s crust cools and hardens completely

quiz.q: Which of these regions is most likely to experience frequent earthquakes and volcanic activity?
options: A region unrelated to plate boundaries
An area with no connection to plate boundaries at all
An area located far from any plate boundary
An area located along a tectonic plate boundary

quiz.q: Why do scientists monitor volcanoes and fault lines?
options: To help predict potential eruptions or earthquakes and protect nearby communities
A reason unrelated to Earth science
Volcanoes and fault lines never pose any risk to communities
Monitoring these areas provides no useful information

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The War of 1812: Causes and Consequences
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the War of 1812 was fought between the United States and Britain (with its colonies, including present-day Canada), shaping early Canadian identity and border relations.

quiz.q: The War of 1812 was fought between the United States and ___.
options: France, alone, with no other countries involved
Spain, alone, with no other countries involved
A country unrelated to the War of 1812
Britain, including its colonies such as present-day Canada

quiz.q: Which of these was a cause of the War of 1812?
options: Trade restrictions and disputes over territory and shipping rights
A complete absence of any tension between the countries
A cause unrelated to the War of 1812
A disagreement about a sporting event

quiz.q: How did the War of 1812 affect the identity of early Canadians?
options: It helped strengthen a shared sense of identity in defending their homeland
A reason unrelated to Canadian identity
It had no effect on how Canadians viewed themselves
It caused Canada to become part of the United States

quiz.q: Which of these was a result of the War of 1812?
options: All trade between the two nations ended permanently
The border between the United States and British North America remained largely unchanged
A result unrelated to the War of 1812
The United States gained full control over all of British North America

quiz.q: Why is the War of 1812 an important event in Canadian history?
options: It played a role in shaping early Canadian identity and relations with the United States
The war had no lasting effects of any kind
The War of 1812 has no connection to Canadian history
A reason unrelated to its historical significance

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Correcting Run-on Sentences and Comma Splices
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: a run-on sentence incorrectly joins two independent clauses with no punctuation, while a comma splice incorrectly joins them with only a comma; both can be fixed with a period, semicolon, or conjunction.

quiz.q: A run-on sentence occurs when ___.
options: A sentence has only one clause
A concept unrelated to run-on sentences
Two independent clauses are joined with no punctuation
A sentence is too short to make sense

quiz.q: A comma splice occurs when ___.
options: A sentence uses too many commas correctly
Two independent clauses are joined by only a comma
A concept unrelated to comma splices
A sentence has no clauses at all

quiz.q: Which is a correct way to fix a comma splice?
options: Remove all punctuation from the sentence
A method unrelated to fixing comma splices
Replace the comma with a semicolon or add a conjunction

quiz.q: Which sentence is a run-on?
options: I finished my homework I went outside to play
I finished my homework, and I went outside to play.
I finished my homework before I went outside.

quiz.q: Why is it important to fix run-on sentences and comma splices?
options: Run-on sentences are always the correct way to write
These errors never affect a reader’s understanding
They can confuse readers about where one idea ends and another begins

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Order of Operations with Integers and Exponents (BEDMAS Deeper Dive)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: the order of operations (BEDMAS) determines the sequence for evaluating expressions with brackets, exponents, division, multiplication, addition, and subtraction, including those with integers.

quiz.q: BEDMAS stands for the order: ___.
Evaluate: 3 + 4 x 2.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Evaluate: (2 + 3)².
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Evaluate: -2 + (-3) x 2.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why must operations be completed in a specific order?
options: Order of operations never affects the result of a calculation
Expressions can be solved in any order with the same result
Without a consistent order, the same expression could give multiple different, incorrect answers

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Magnetism and Electromagnets
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: magnetism is a force that attracts certain materials, such as iron, and an electromagnet is a temporary magnet created when electric current flows through a coiled wire.

quiz.q: Magnetism is a force that attracts materials such as ___.
An electromagnet is created when ___.
options: A permanent magnet is placed in water
A material is heated to a very high temperature
Electric current flows through a coiled wire

quiz.q: Unlike a permanent magnet, an electromagnet’s magnetism can be ___.
options: Increased only by cooling it down
Turned on and off by controlling the electric current

quiz.q: Which of these could increase the strength of an electromagnet?
options: Using a material that does not conduct electricity
Increasing the number of coils of wire

quiz.q: Why are electromagnets useful in devices like electric motors and doorbells?
options: Their magnetism can be controlled and adjusted using electricity
Electromagnets cannot be used in any electrical device
Electromagnets provide no practical use in technology

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Red River Resistance and Louis Riel
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the Red River Resistance was led by Louis Riel and the Métis people to protect their land, rights, and way of life as Canada expanded westward in the late 1800s.

quiz.q: The Red River Resistance was led by ___.
options: A group with no connection to the Métis
Louis Riel and the Métis people
A group unrelated to the Red River Resistance
A group opposed to protecting any land rights

quiz.q: The Red River Resistance occurred largely in response to ___.
options: Canada’s westward expansion threatening Métis land and rights
A cause unrelated to the Red River Resistance
A sudden decrease in Canada’s population

quiz.q: The Métis people are often described as having ___.
options: Ancestry entirely unrelated to Canada’s history
No connection to Indigenous or European heritage

quiz.q: Why is Louis Riel considered a significant figure in Canadian history?
options: He led efforts to protect Métis rights and land during a period of major change
He opposed all forms of Métis representation
A reason unrelated to his historical significance
He played no role in Canadian history

quiz.q: Why do students study the Red River Resistance today?
options: It highlights the struggles for Indigenous and Métis rights during Canada’s expansion
This event has no connection to understanding Canadian history
A reason unrelated to social studies learning
The Red River Resistance never actually took place

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Homophones and Homographs
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: homophones are words that sound alike but have different meanings and spellings, while homographs are spelled the same but may have different meanings and pronunciations.

quiz.q: Homophones are words that ___.
options: Have the exact same meaning as one another
Are spelled identically but sound different
Sound alike but have different meanings and spellings

quiz.q: Which pair of words are homophones?
A homograph is a word that ___.
options: Always sounds completely different from other words
Is spelled the same as another word but may differ in meaning or pronunciation

quiz.q: Which word is a homograph, having two different pronunciations and meanings?
Why can homophones be tricky for writers?
options: Homophones never cause any confusion in writing
They are easy to confuse because they sound the same but are spelled differently

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Volume and Surface Area of Pyramids and Cones
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: the volume of a pyramid or cone is one-third the volume of a prism or cylinder with the same base and height, and their surface area includes the base plus the slanted lateral surface.

quiz.q: The volume of a pyramid or cone is ___ the volume of a prism or cylinder with the same base and height.
options: A fraction unrelated to this relationship

quiz.q: The formula for the volume of a cone is ___.
options: A formula unrelated to the volume of a cone

quiz.q: The surface area of a pyramid includes ___.
options: Only the base, with no other surfaces
A concept unrelated to surface area
Only the height of the pyramid
The base plus the area of its triangular faces

quiz.q: If a cone and a cylinder share the same base and height, and the cylinder’s volume is 90 cm³, what is the cone’s volume?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why might understanding cone and pyramid volume be useful in real life?
options: It helps calculate the capacity of objects like ice cream cones or tents
These calculations have no real-world use
Cones and pyramids never have a measurable volume

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Greenhouse Effect and Global Climate Change
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: the greenhouse effect occurs when gases in the atmosphere trap heat from the sun, and an increase in these gases from human activity is linked to global climate change.

quiz.q: The greenhouse effect occurs when gases in the atmosphere ___.
options: Trap heat from the sun near the Earth’s surface
A concept unrelated to the greenhouse effect
Block all sunlight from reaching the Earth
Remove all heat from the atmosphere

quiz.q: Which of these is considered a greenhouse gas?
options: A gas unrelated to the greenhouse effect

quiz.q: Which human activity is commonly linked to increased greenhouse gas emissions?
options: An activity unrelated to greenhouse gases

quiz.q: A natural greenhouse effect is important because it ___.
options: A reason unrelated to the greenhouse effect
Has no effect on Earth’s temperature
Keeps the Earth warm enough to support life
Makes the Earth too cold to support life

quiz.q: Why are scientists concerned about an increase in greenhouse gases from human activity?
options: Increased greenhouse gases have no effect on global temperatures
Greenhouse gases only affect a single, isolated location
A reason unrelated to climate change
It can lead to rising global temperatures and significant climate changes

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Territorial Evolution: How the Provinces and Territories Joined
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: Canada’s provinces and territories joined Confederation at different times, gradually expanding the country’s borders from the original four provinces in 1867 to the ten provinces and three territories of today.

quiz.q: Canada began Confederation in 1867 with how many original provinces?
Canada’s territorial evolution refers to ___.
options: A concept unrelated to Canada’s territorial evolution
How provinces and territories gradually joined the country over time
A single event where all provinces joined at once
A process unrelated to Canada’s formation

quiz.q: Why might a region have chosen to join Confederation?
options: Regions were required to remain isolated forever
No benefit was ever offered to any region
Economic, political, or security benefits of joining a larger country

quiz.q: How many provinces and territories make up Canada today?
options: A number unrelated to Canada today

quiz.q: Why is it useful to study how Canada’s provinces and territories joined over time?
options: It helps explain the country’s political development and regional differences
This history has no connection to understanding Canada today
Canada’s borders have never changed since 1867
A reason unrelated to social studies learning

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Internal and External Conflict
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: internal conflict is a struggle within a character’s own mind, while external conflict is a struggle between a character and an outside force, such as another character or nature.

quiz.q: Internal conflict is a struggle ___.
options: A concept unrelated to internal conflict
Between a character and the weather

quiz.q: External conflict is a struggle between a character and ___.
options: An outside force, such as another character or nature
Only their own thoughts and feelings
A concept unrelated to external conflict

quiz.q: Which is an example of internal conflict?
options: A character struggling to decide between telling the truth or a lie
A character fighting against a storm at sea
A concept unrelated to internal conflict
Two characters arguing over a game

quiz.q: Which is an example of external conflict?
options: A concept unrelated to external conflict
A character competing against a rival in a race
A character silently doubting their own abilities

quiz.q: Why might an author include both internal and external conflict in a story?
options: A reason unrelated to narrative writing
It adds depth by showing a character’s inner struggles alongside outside challenges
Stories are always more interesting with no conflict at all
Conflict never adds depth to a story

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Probability: Combinations and Permutations (Intro)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: a permutation counts the number of ways items can be arranged when order matters, while a combination counts the number of ways items can be selected when order does not matter.

quiz.q: A permutation counts arrangements where ___.
options: Only one arrangement is ever possible
Order does not matter at all

quiz.q: A combination counts selections where ___.
How many ways can you arrange the letters A, B, and C in order?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: If you are choosing 2 fruits from a bowl of apples, bananas, and oranges, and order does not matter, this is an example of a ___.
options: A concept unrelated to this scenario

quiz.q: Why might the number of permutations of a set be larger than the number of combinations of the same set?
options: Combinations always count more possibilities than permutations
Permutations count every different order separately, while combinations group the same items together regardless of order
Permutations and combinations always produce the exact same count

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Density and Buoyancy
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: density is the amount of mass in a given volume of a substance, and buoyancy explains why objects less dense than a fluid float while denser objects sink.

quiz.q: Density is defined as ___.
options: The total weight of an object with no reference to volume
The amount of mass in a given volume of a substance

quiz.q: An object will float in water if it is ___.
options: Exactly the same temperature as water

quiz.q: An object will sink in water if it is ___.
Which formula correctly represents density?
Why can a large steel ship float, even though steel is denser than water?
options: Ships are not affected by buoyancy at all
Its overall shape displaces enough water to make its average density less than water
Steel always floats regardless of its shape

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Human Rights: The Universal Declaration and Canadian Protections
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the Universal Declaration of Human Rights outlines fundamental rights meant to be protected for all people, and Canada upholds many of these rights through its own laws and protections.

quiz.q: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights outlines rights meant to be protected for ___.
options: Only citizens of a single country
A group unrelated to human rights

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a fundamental human right?
options: A concept unrelated to human rights
The right to freedom of expression
The right to ignore all laws
The right to control every other person’s beliefs

quiz.q: Canada protects many human rights through laws such as ___.
options: A law that removes protections for citizens
A law unrelated to Canadian rights protections
A law with no connection to human rights
The Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms

quiz.q: Why was the Universal Declaration of Human Rights created after World War II?
options: To help prevent future atrocities by establishing shared international standards for human dignity
A reason unrelated to its historical origins
It was created with no connection to global events
It aimed to reduce cooperation between countries

quiz.q: Why is it valuable for students to learn about human rights?
options: Human rights have no connection to understanding society
It helps them understand the protections and responsibilities that support a fair society
A reason unrelated to social studies learning
Human rights are a concept limited to only one country

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Analyzing Memes and Internet Culture
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: memes use images, text, and humour to quickly spread ideas online, and analyzing them involves considering their intended message, audience, and potential impact.

quiz.q: A meme typically combines ___.
options: Silence with no message at all
Only long paragraphs of formal text
Images, text, and humour to spread an idea quickly

quiz.q: When analyzing a meme, it is useful to consider ___.
options: A concept unrelated to analyzing memes
Only how many colours it uses
Its intended message, audience, and potential impact
Nothing, since memes have no meaning

quiz.q: Why can memes spread ideas quickly online?
options: Memes are always difficult to understand quickly
A reason unrelated to internet culture
They are easy to share, understand, and relate to at a glance

quiz.q: Which is a potential concern when analyzing internet culture and memes?
options: Internet culture has no connection to media literacy
Memes can spread misinformation or biased ideas quickly
A concept unrelated to media literacy
Memes never influence how people think

quiz.q: Why is media literacy important when engaging with internet culture?
options: It helps people think critically about the messages behind online content
Critical thinking is never needed online
All online content is equally accurate and reliable
A reason unrelated to media literacy

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Algebra: Solving Equations Involving Fractions and Decimals
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: solving equations involving fractions or decimals often involves multiplying through by a common denominator or power of ten to clear the fractions or decimals before isolating the variable.

quiz.q: One strategy for solving an equation with fractions is to ___.
options: Multiply every term by a common denominator to clear the fractions
A concept unrelated to solving equations

quiz.q: Solve for x: x/2 + 3 = 7.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Solve for x: 0.5x = 10.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Solve for x: x/3 = 4.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why might clearing fractions or decimals make an equation easier to solve?
options: Fractions and decimals never affect how an equation is solved
It allows the equation to be solved using simpler whole-number operations
Clearing fractions or decimals always makes an equation harder to solve
A reason unrelated to solving equations

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Invasive Species and Their Impact on Ecosystems
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: an invasive species is a non-native organism introduced to an ecosystem, often causing harm by outcompeting native species for resources.

quiz.q: An invasive species is a ___.
options: Non-native organism introduced to an ecosystem
Native organism that has always lived in an ecosystem
Organism that has no effect on any ecosystem
A concept unrelated to invasive species

quiz.q: Invasive species often harm ecosystems by ___.
options: Having no interaction with native species at all
A concept unrelated to invasive species
Providing extra resources for every native species
Outcompeting native species for food, space, or resources

quiz.q: Which of these is a way an invasive species might be introduced to a new ecosystem?
options: A method unrelated to species introduction
Evolving naturally within that same ecosystem over time
Accidentally transported through international shipping or trade

quiz.q: Why can invasive species be especially difficult to control once established?
options: Invasive species always disappear naturally within a few days
A reason unrelated to invasive species
Invasive species are never able to survive in a new environment
They often lack natural predators in their new environment

quiz.q: Why do scientists study the impact of invasive species on ecosystems?
options: Invasive species have no real impact on ecosystems worth studying
To better understand and manage the risks they pose to native biodiversity
Studying invasive species provides no useful environmental information

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Electoral System and the Voting Process
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: Canada’s electoral system allows citizens to vote for representatives in federal, provincial, and municipal elections, playing a key role in how government leaders are chosen.

quiz.q: Canada’s electoral system allows citizens to ___.
options: A concept unrelated to Canada’s electoral system
Choose their own laws with no representatives at all
Vote for representatives in government elections
Vote only once in their entire lifetime

quiz.q: In a federal election, Canadians vote for a representative in their ___.
options: A concept unrelated to federal elections

quiz.q: Which level of government holds elections for a mayor and city council?
options: No level of government holds these elections
A level of government unrelated to city elections

quiz.q: Why is voting considered an important civic responsibility?
options: Citizens have no role in choosing government leaders
It allows citizens to have a voice in choosing their government representatives
Voting has no effect on who leads the government
A reason unrelated to civic responsibility

quiz.q: Why might it be important for citizens to understand the voting process before an election?
options: The voting process is not open to input from citizens
Understanding the voting process has no effect on participation
It helps ensure they can participate confidently and effectively in choosing their representatives

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a Public Service Announcement (PSA) Script
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: a public service announcement (PSA) script delivers a brief, persuasive message about an important issue, using clear language and a strong call to action aimed at a specific audience.

quiz.q: A public service announcement (PSA) is designed to ___.
options: Tell a long fictional story with no clear message
Sell a specific product for profit
Deliver a brief, persuasive message about an important issue

quiz.q: A strong PSA typically includes a clear ___.
Why should a PSA be written for a specific audience?
options: Audience has no effect on how a PSA is written
A reason unrelated to writing a PSA
PSAs are always exactly the same for every audience
Tailoring the message helps it connect and resonate with that audience

quiz.q: Which is an appropriate topic for a PSA?
options: Encouraging people to recycle to protect the environment
A private diary entry with no public message
A fictional adventure story with dragons
A concept unrelated to PSA topics

quiz.q: Why is concise, clear language important in a PSA script?
options: PSAs are often short, so the message must be understood quickly
Clarity is never important in this type of writing
PSAs are always very long with no time limit
A reason unrelated to writing a PSA

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Data: Line Graphs and Analyzing Trends Over Time
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: a line graph displays how data changes over time, and analyzing its trend involves identifying whether values are increasing, decreasing, or staying constant.

quiz.q: A line graph is especially useful for showing ___.
options: Data with no connection to time at all
Only a single data point with no comparison
A concept unrelated to line graphs

quiz.q: If a line graph slopes upward from left to right, the trend is ___.
options: A trend unrelated to this description

quiz.q: If a line graph slopes downward from left to right, the trend is ___.
options: A trend unrelated to this description

quiz.q: A flat, horizontal section of a line graph indicates that the data is ___.
options: A concept unrelated to line graphs

quiz.q: Why might a business analyze trends on a line graph of monthly sales?
options: It helps identify patterns that can inform future planning and decisions
Line graphs cannot be used to analyze sales data
Trends on a line graph provide no useful information
A reason unrelated to analyzing data

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Biotechnology and Genetic Engineering (Intro)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: biotechnology uses living organisms or their processes to develop useful products, and genetic engineering involves directly modifying an organism’s DNA for a specific purpose.

quiz.q: Biotechnology uses ___.
options: A process unrelated to living organisms
Living organisms or their processes to develop useful products
Only non-living materials with no biological component

quiz.q: Genetic engineering involves ___.
options: A concept unrelated to genetic engineering
Studying an organism’s DNA with no modifications at all
Directly modifying an organism’s DNA for a specific purpose
Removing all DNA from an organism

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a biotechnology application?
options: Painting a picture of a landscape
Developing crops that are more resistant to disease
Building a bridge using steel and concrete

quiz.q: Why might scientists genetically engineer certain crops?
options: Genetic engineering has no effect on a crop’s traits
A reason unrelated to genetic engineering
To make crops less nutritious on purpose
To improve traits like disease resistance or nutritional value

quiz.q: Why is biotechnology an important and sometimes debated topic in science?
options: It raises both potential benefits and ethical questions about modifying living organisms
Biotechnology has no real-world applications or implications
Biotechnology is a topic with no connection to ethics or society

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Income Inequality and Wealth Distribution
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: income inequality refers to the uneven distribution of income and wealth within a society, and studying it helps explain economic challenges and opportunities faced by different groups.

quiz.q: Income inequality refers to ___.
options: A concept unrelated to income inequality
A society where everyone earns exactly the same amount
The uneven distribution of income and wealth within a society

quiz.q: Which of these could be a cause of income inequality?
options: Every person having identical access to resources
A cause unrelated to economic factors
Unequal access to education and job opportunities
A cause unrelated to income inequality

quiz.q: Which of these is a potential effect of significant income inequality?
options: An effect unrelated to income inequality
Complete economic equality for all citizens
No effect on any aspect of society
Unequal access to healthcare, housing, or education

quiz.q: Which of these is a policy some governments use to help address income inequality?
options: Progressive taxation, where higher earners pay a higher tax rate
A policy unrelated to reducing inequality
A policy unrelated to income or taxation
A policy that increases inequality on purpose

quiz.q: Why is income inequality an important topic to study in social studies?
options: Income inequality is a concern only in fictional scenarios
Income inequality has no connection to social studies topics
It connects to economics, fairness, and the well-being of communities
A reason unrelated to this unit

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Using Transitional Phrases to Link Ideas
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: transitional phrases, such as “in addition” or “as a result,” connect ideas within and between paragraphs, helping writing flow logically and clearly.

quiz.q: Transitional phrases help writing by ___.
options: Removing the need for paragraphs entirely
Connecting ideas logically within and between paragraphs
Making ideas more confusing and disconnected
A concept unrelated to transitional phrases

quiz.q: Which phrase could signal a cause-and-effect relationship?
options: A phrase unrelated to cause and effect

quiz.q: Which phrase could signal a contrast between two ideas?
options: A phrase unrelated to signalling contrast

quiz.q: Which phrase could be used to add a similar or additional idea?
options: A phrase unrelated to adding ideas

quiz.q: Why might a writer use transitional phrases throughout an essay?
options: Transitional phrases always make an essay harder to follow
A reason unrelated to transitional phrases
Ideas never need to be connected in writing
They guide the reader smoothly from one idea to the next

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Financial Literacy: Income, Expenses, and Net Worth
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: net worth is calculated by subtracting total expenses or debts from total income or assets, helping individuals understand and track their overall financial position.

quiz.q: Net worth is calculated by ___.
options: A concept unrelated to net worth
Subtracting total debts from total assets
Multiplying income by a fixed rate

quiz.q: Income refers to ___.
options: Money spent on goods and services
A type of debt owed to a bank
Money earned or received, such as from a job

quiz.q: Expenses refer to ___.
options: Money spent on goods, services, or bills

quiz.q: If a person has $5,000 in assets and $2,000 in debts, what is their net worth?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why is tracking income and expenses important for financial literacy?
options: Net worth has no connection to income or expenses
It helps individuals understand their financial position and make informed spending decisions
A reason unrelated to financial literacy
Tracking income and expenses provides no useful financial information

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Groundwater and the Water Table
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: groundwater is water stored beneath the Earth’s surface in soil and rock, and the water table marks the upper boundary of the area saturated with this water.

quiz.q: Groundwater is water that is stored ___.
options: In clouds high in the atmosphere
Only on the surface of oceans and lakes
Beneath the Earth’s surface, in soil and rock

quiz.q: The water table marks ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the water table
The exact centre of the Earth
The upper boundary of the area saturated with groundwater

quiz.q: Which of these could cause the water table to drop?
options: A cause unrelated to the water table
A significant increase in annual rainfall
Excessive pumping of groundwater for human use
A process unrelated to groundwater levels

quiz.q: A well is typically drilled deep enough to reach ___.
options: Only the very surface of the soil
A location with no connection to groundwater
Below the water table, where groundwater is present

quiz.q: Why is groundwater an important resource to protect from pollution?
options: Groundwater has no connection to drinking water supplies
Many communities rely on it as a source of drinking water

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Urban vs. Rural Life in Canada
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: urban areas are densely populated with access to many services, while rural areas have lower population density and often rely more on agriculture or natural resources, creating distinct lifestyles across Canada.

quiz.q: Urban areas are generally characterized by ___.
options: Very low populations with few services
A complete absence of any people
Dense populations and many available services
A concept unrelated to urban areas

quiz.q: Rural areas often rely more heavily on ___.
options: A concept unrelated to rural economies
A resource unrelated to rural economies

quiz.q: Which of these might be a challenge faced by people living in rural areas?
options: A challenge unrelated to rural areas
Longer travel distances to access certain services
An overwhelming number of nearby services
No connection to distance or access at all

quiz.q: Which of these might be a challenge faced by people living in urban areas?
options: Higher cost of living and traffic congestion
A complete absence of any nearby services
A challenge unrelated to urban areas

quiz.q: Why is it useful to compare urban and rural life in Canada?
options: Comparing these areas has no relevance to social studies
Urban and rural life are always identical in every way
A reason unrelated to social studies learning
It helps explain how geography and population affect people’s daily lives and opportunities

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Evaluating an Author’s Credentials and Expertise
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: evaluating an author’s credentials and expertise involves considering their background, training, and experience to judge how reliable their claims about a topic might be.

quiz.q: An author’s credentials refer to their ___.
options: A concept unrelated to author credentials
Background, training, and experience related to a topic
Favourite hobbies with no connection to the topic

quiz.q: Why might a reader check an author’s expertise before trusting their claims?
options: A reason unrelated to evaluating sources
An author’s background never affects how reliable their claims are
Expertise can indicate how reliable and well-informed the author’s claims are likely to be
All authors are equally reliable regardless of expertise

quiz.q: Which is an example of a credential that might support an author’s expertise on a medical topic?
options: A concept unrelated to medical expertise
Years of experience working as a doctor
No connection to the medical field at all

quiz.q: Why might a text written by someone with no relevant expertise be viewed with more caution?
options: The claims may be less informed or less reliable without proper background knowledge
Expertise never matters when evaluating a text
A reason unrelated to evaluating a text
A lack of expertise always makes a text more trustworthy

quiz.q: Why is evaluating an author’s credentials an important reading skill?
options: It helps readers judge how much trust to place in a text’s claims
This skill has no effect on understanding a text
A reason unrelated to reading comprehension
Every author’s claims should be trusted equally without question

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Geometry: Constructing Triangles and Angle Bisectors
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: triangles can be constructed using given side lengths or angles, and an angle bisector is a line that divides an angle into two equal parts.

quiz.q: An angle bisector divides an angle into ___.
options: A concept unrelated to angle bisectors

quiz.q: To construct a triangle given three side lengths, a compass is often used to ___.
options: Draw a circle unrelated to the triangle
A concept unrelated to triangle construction
Measure the temperature of the triangle
Mark equal distances from given points

quiz.q: If an angle measures 60 degrees, its bisector creates two angles of ___ each.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Which set of side lengths could NOT form a triangle?
options: A set unrelated to triangle construction

quiz.q: Why might construction tools like a compass and straightedge be used in geometry?
options: These tools never help with accuracy in geometry
A reason unrelated to geometric construction
Constructions can always be done more accurately by estimating
They allow for precise, accurate constructions of shapes and angles

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Integumentary System: Skin, Hair, and Nails
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: the integumentary system, made up of the skin, hair, and nails, protects the body from injury and infection while helping regulate body temperature.

quiz.q: The integumentary system is made up of the skin, hair, and ___.
options: A structure unrelated to the integumentary system

quiz.q: One main function of the skin is to ___.
options: A function unrelated to the skin
Protect the body from injury and infection

quiz.q: The skin helps regulate body temperature through processes such as ___.
options: A process unrelated to temperature regulation

quiz.q: Which of these is a function of hair on the human body?
options: Breaking down food in the stomach

quiz.q: Why is the integumentary system considered the body’s first line of defence?
options: A reason unrelated to the integumentary system
This system has no protective function at all
It forms a protective barrier between the body and the outside environment
The integumentary system is located entirely inside the body

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Mining and Natural Resource Industries
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: Canada’s mining and natural resource industries extract valuable materials like minerals and metals, playing a significant role in the economy while raising important questions about environmental impact.

quiz.q: Canada’s mining industry primarily focuses on extracting ___.
Why are mining and natural resource industries significant to Canada’s economy?
options: These industries provide no economic benefit at all
They have no connection to Canada’s economy
They provide jobs and generate significant economic activity
A reason unrelated to Canada’s economy

quiz.q: Which of these is a potential environmental concern related to mining?
options: Mining always improves the surrounding environment
Disruption of ecosystems and habitats near mining sites
Mining has no possible environmental impact

quiz.q: Why might governments regulate mining and resource extraction?
options: A reason unrelated to resource regulation
Regulations provide no benefit to the environment or communities
Mining requires no regulation of any kind
To help balance economic benefits with environmental and community protection

quiz.q: Why is it useful for students to learn about Canada’s natural resource industries?
options: Natural resource industries have no connection to understanding Canada
A reason unrelated to social studies learning
It helps them understand economic development alongside environmental responsibility
This topic is not relevant to social studies learning

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Review: Language Skills (Days 61-69)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 61-69, including author’s purpose, business letters, run-on sentences, homophones, conflict, memes, PSAs, transitions, and author credibility.

quiz.q: An author’s purpose for writing is often to ___.
options: Confuse readers with no clear goal
A concept unrelated to author’s purpose

quiz.q: A run-on sentence occurs when ___.
options: A sentence is too short to make sense
A concept unrelated to run-on sentences
A sentence has only one clause
Two independent clauses are joined with no punctuation

quiz.q: Internal conflict is a struggle ___.
options: A concept unrelated to internal conflict
Between a character and the weather

quiz.q: Transitional phrases help writing by ___.
options: A concept unrelated to transitional phrases
Removing the need for paragraphs entirely
Connecting ideas logically within and between paragraphs
Making ideas more confusing and disconnected

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review purpose, structure, grammar, and evaluation skills together?
options: It reinforces how these language skills work together for effective reading and writing
Review never helps strengthen understanding of language
These skills have no connection to each other
A reason unrelated to reviewing language skills

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Review: Math Skills (Days 61-69)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 61-69, including fraction operations, order of operations, pyramid and cone volume, combinations and permutations, and financial literacy.

quiz.q: What is 1/4 + 1/2?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Evaluate: 3 + 4 x 2.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: A permutation counts arrangements where ___.
options: Only one arrangement is ever possible
Order does not matter at all

quiz.q: Net worth is calculated by ___.
options: Multiplying income by a fixed rate
A concept unrelated to net worth
Subtracting total debts from total assets

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review fraction operations, order of operations, geometry, probability, and finance together?
options: These topics have no connection to each other
A reason unrelated to reviewing math
It reinforces how these math concepts connect and build on one another
Review never helps strengthen understanding of math

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Review: Science Skills (Days 61-69)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 61-69, including the nervous system, volcanoes and earthquakes, magnetism, the greenhouse effect, and biotechnology.

quiz.q: The nervous system is primarily made up of the brain, spinal cord, and ___.
options: A structure unrelated to the nervous system

quiz.q: Volcanoes and earthquakes are often caused by ___.
options: A sudden increase in ocean temperature
A concept unrelated to volcanoes and earthquakes

quiz.q: The greenhouse effect occurs when gases in the atmosphere ___.
options: Trap heat from the sun near the Earth’s surface
Remove all heat from the atmosphere
A concept unrelated to the greenhouse effect
Block all sunlight from reaching the Earth

quiz.q: Genetic engineering involves ___.
options: Directly modifying an organism’s DNA for a specific purpose
Studying an organism’s DNA with no modifications at all
Removing all DNA from an organism
A concept unrelated to genetic engineering

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review body systems, Earth processes, and emerging technologies together?
options: It reinforces how these interconnected science concepts relate to one another
These topics have no connection to each other
Review is never useful in science
A reason unrelated to reviewing science

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Review: Social Studies Skills (Days 61-69)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 61-69, including the fur trade, the War of 1812, the Red River Resistance, Confederation’s expansion, human rights, and Canada’s electoral system.

quiz.q: The fur trade was primarily built on the exchange of ___.
options: Furs, especially beaver pelts, for goods
A concept unrelated to the fur trade

quiz.q: The Red River Resistance was led by ___.
options: A group unrelated to the Red River Resistance
A group with no connection to the Métis
Louis Riel and the Métis people
A group opposed to protecting any land rights

quiz.q: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights outlines rights meant to be protected for ___.
options: A group unrelated to human rights
Only citizens of a single country

quiz.q: Canada’s electoral system allows citizens to ___.
options: Choose their own laws with no representatives at all
A concept unrelated to Canada’s electoral system
Vote only once in their entire lifetime
Vote for representatives in government elections

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review Canadian history, rights, and government together?
options: A reason unrelated to reviewing social studies
Review is never useful when studying history and government
These topics have no connection to one another
It reinforces how these interconnected historical and political topics shaped modern Canada

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Point of View: First-Person, Third-Person Limited, and Omniscient Narration
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: point of view describes who is telling a story — first-person narrators use I, third-person limited narrators reveal one character’s thoughts, and third-person omniscient narrators know the thoughts of every character.

quiz.q: A first-person narrator tells a story using ___.
options: A concept unrelated to point of view
Only third-person pronouns like he and she
Pronouns such as I and we

quiz.q: A third-person limited narrator reveals the thoughts of ___.
options: A concept unrelated to third-person limited narration

quiz.q: A third-person omniscient narrator ___.
options: Can only describe what one character sees
Knows the thoughts and feelings of every character
Has no knowledge of any character’s thoughts
A concept unrelated to omniscient narration

quiz.q: Which sentence is told from a first-person point of view?
options: He and she walked toward the house together.
I walked slowly toward the old house.
A sentence unrelated to point of view
She walked slowly toward the old house.

quiz.q: Why might an author choose a limited point of view instead of an omniscient one?
options: It can build suspense by keeping some information hidden from the reader
Omniscient narration always reveals less information than limited narration
A reason unrelated to point of view
Point of view never affects how a reader experiences a story

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Circle Graphs (Pie Charts): Constructing and Interpreting
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: a circle graph (pie chart) represents data as proportional slices of a circle, where each slice’s size corresponds to its percentage of the whole data set.

quiz.q: A circle graph represents data using ___.
options: A concept unrelated to circle graphs

quiz.q: In a circle graph, a larger slice represents ___.
options: A smaller percentage of the total data
A greater percentage of the total data
No connection to the data at all
A concept unrelated to circle graphs

quiz.q: If a circle graph shows that 50% of students chose pizza as their favourite food, that slice would take up ___ of the circle.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: A full circle graph always represents ___.
options: A value unrelated to circle graphs

quiz.q: Why might a circle graph be a good choice for displaying survey results?
options: Circle graphs can only display exactly two categories
Circle graphs never show proportions clearly
It clearly shows how each category compares as a proportion of the whole
A reason unrelated to displaying data

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Introduction to Atoms, Elements, and the Periodic Table
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: atoms are the basic building blocks of matter, elements are pure substances made of only one type of atom, and the periodic table organizes all known elements by their properties.

quiz.q: An atom is ___.
options: The basic building block of matter
A mixture of two or more substances

quiz.q: An element is a pure substance made of ___.
options: Two or more different types of atoms combined

quiz.q: The periodic table organizes elements by their ___.
options: Properties, such as atomic structure and behaviour
A concept unrelated to the periodic table

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of an element?
Why is the periodic table a useful tool for scientists?
options: The periodic table provides no useful scientific information
A reason unrelated to the periodic table
It organizes elements in a way that reveals patterns in their properties
Elements on the periodic table share no patterns at all

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Acadian Expulsion (Le Grand Dérangement)
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the Acadian Expulsion, known as Le Grand Dérangement, was the forced removal of thousands of Acadian settlers from their homes by the British in the 1750s.

quiz.q: The Acadian Expulsion involved the forced removal of ___.
options: A small group of British soldiers
Thousands of Acadian settlers from their homes
A concept unrelated to the Acadian Expulsion

quiz.q: The Acadian Expulsion was carried out by ___.
options: A group unrelated to the Acadian Expulsion

quiz.q: Le Grand Dérangement took place primarily during the ___.
options: A time period unrelated to the Acadian Expulsion

quiz.q: Many Acadians who were expelled were forced to ___.
options: Remain safely in their original homes
Resettle in distant and unfamiliar regions
A consequence unrelated to the Acadian Expulsion

quiz.q: Why is the Acadian Expulsion an important event to study in Canadian history?
options: The Acadian Expulsion had no lasting impact on Canadian history
A reason unrelated to its historical significance
This event never actually took place
It highlights the impact of colonial conflict on the lives of settler communities

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a Short Story with a Clear Plot Arc
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: a short story with a clear plot arc follows a structure of exposition, rising action, climax, falling action, and resolution to build tension and satisfy the reader.

quiz.q: The exposition of a story introduces ___.
options: The final resolution of the conflict
The characters, setting, and initial situation

quiz.q: The climax of a story is ___.
options: A concept unrelated to a story’s climax
The very first event in the story
A part of the story with no importance
The turning point or moment of highest tension

quiz.q: Rising action refers to the events that ___.
options: Occur only in the story’s final sentence
Build tension and lead toward the climax
Happen after the story’s conflict is resolved
A concept unrelated to rising action

quiz.q: The resolution of a story typically ___.
options: Introduces the main characters for the first time
A concept unrelated to a story’s resolution
Shows how the conflict is finally settled

quiz.q: Why is a clear plot arc useful when writing a short story?
options: It helps organize events so the story builds tension and feels satisfying to readers
A reason unrelated to writing a short story
Stories are always more effective with no structure at all
A plot arc never affects how a story is experienced

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Repeating and Terminating Decimals
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: a terminating decimal ends after a finite number of digits, while a repeating decimal has one or more digits that repeat infinitely, and both can result from converting fractions to decimal form.

quiz.q: A terminating decimal is a decimal that ___.
options: Ends after a finite number of digits
Has no digits after the decimal point
A concept unrelated to terminating decimals

quiz.q: A repeating decimal is a decimal in which ___.
options: The digits always end after two places
No digits ever appear after the decimal point
A concept unrelated to repeating decimals
One or more digits repeat infinitely

quiz.q: Which fraction converts to a terminating decimal?
options: A fraction unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Which fraction converts to a repeating decimal?
options: A fraction unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why is it useful to recognize whether a decimal terminates or repeats?
options: This distinction never matters when working with decimals
All decimals terminate after exactly one digit
It helps identify patterns when converting between fractions and decimals

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Static Electricity and Electric Charge
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: static electricity builds up when electric charges accumulate on the surface of an object, often due to friction, and can cause objects to attract or repel one another.

quiz.q: Static electricity builds up when ___.
options: A concept unrelated to static electricity
A magnet is placed near metal
Electric charges accumulate on the surface of an object
An object loses all of its mass

quiz.q: Static electricity is often caused by ___.
options: A concept unrelated to static electricity

quiz.q: Objects with the same electric charge will ___ each other.
options: A concept unrelated to electric charge

quiz.q: Objects with opposite electric charges will ___ each other.
options: A concept unrelated to electric charge

quiz.q: Why does rubbing a balloon on hair sometimes cause it to stick to a wall?
options: A reason unrelated to static electricity
Balloons always stick to walls regardless of electric charge
The friction transfers electric charge, creating an attraction between the balloon and the wall
Friction removes all electric charge from the balloon

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Underground Railroad and Its Connection to Canada
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the Underground Railroad was a secret network of routes and safe houses that helped freedom seekers escape enslavement in the United States, with many settling in British North America, including present-day Ontario.

quiz.q: The Underground Railroad was a secret network that helped ___.
options: Settlers travel to Europe for trade
A concept unrelated to the Underground Railroad
Freedom seekers escape enslavement in the United States

quiz.q: Many freedom seekers who used the Underground Railroad settled in ___.
options: British North America, including present-day Ontario
A region unrelated to the Underground Railroad
Regions with no connection to freedom seekers

quiz.q: The Underground Railroad relied on ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the Underground Railroad
Secret routes and safe houses run by helpers along the way
Official government railways with public schedules

quiz.q: Why did British North America become a destination for freedom seekers?
options: British North America required freedom seekers to remain enslaved
A reason unrelated to the Underground Railroad
Slavery had been abolished there, offering greater safety and freedom
British North America had no laws regarding slavery at all

quiz.q: Why is the Underground Railroad an important part of Canadian history?
options: It highlights Canada’s role as a destination for people escaping enslavement
This network only ever operated within Canada’s borders
The Underground Railroad has no connection to Canadian history
A reason unrelated to its historical significance

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Correcting Pronoun-Antecedent Agreement Errors
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: a pronoun must agree in number with its antecedent, the noun it replaces, to avoid confusing or grammatically incorrect sentences.

quiz.q: An antecedent is ___.
options: The noun that a pronoun refers back to
A verb tense used in formal writing

quiz.q: Which sentence shows correct pronoun-antecedent agreement?
options: The girls packed their bags for the trip.
The girls packed her bags for the trip.
The girl packed their bags for the trip.
A sentence unrelated to pronoun-antecedent agreement

quiz.q: If the antecedent is singular, the pronoun that replaces it must also be ___.
options: A concept unrelated to pronoun-antecedent agreement

quiz.q: Which sentence contains a pronoun-antecedent agreement error?
options: A sentence unrelated to pronoun-antecedent agreement
The book fell off its shelf and landed on the floor.
The books fell off their shelf and landed on the floor.
The book fell off their shelf and landed on the floor.

quiz.q: Why is it important to match a pronoun with its correct antecedent?
options: It helps readers clearly understand which noun the pronoun is referring to
Antecedents have no connection to the pronouns used in a sentence
Pronoun-antecedent agreement never affects how clear a sentence is

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: The Cartesian Plane and Plotting in All Four Quadrants
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: the Cartesian plane is divided into four quadrants by a horizontal x-axis and vertical y-axis, and each point is located using an ordered pair (x, y).

quiz.q: The Cartesian plane is divided into ___ quadrants.
options: A number unrelated to the Cartesian plane

quiz.q: An ordered pair (x, y) identifies a point’s location using ___.
options: A concept unrelated to ordered pairs
Its distance along the x-axis and y-axis
Only its distance from the origin

quiz.q: In which quadrant would the point (-3, 4) be located?
options: A quadrant unrelated to this point

quiz.q: The point where the x-axis and y-axis intersect is called the ___.
options: A term unrelated to the Cartesian plane

quiz.q: Why is the Cartesian plane useful in mathematics?
options: It allows points, shapes, and relationships to be represented and analyzed visually
A reason unrelated to the Cartesian plane
Points can only be described using words, never a plane
The Cartesian plane has no practical mathematical use

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Light: Reflection and the Law of Reflection
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: reflection occurs when light bounces off a surface, and the law of reflection states that the angle at which light hits a surface equals the angle at which it bounces off.

quiz.q: Reflection occurs when ___.
options: Light is completely absorbed by a surface
Light bends as it passes through a new material

quiz.q: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the law of reflection
Has no relationship to the angle of reflection
Is always greater than the angle of reflection

quiz.q: A smooth, shiny surface like a mirror produces a ___ reflection.
A rough surface, such as paper, produces a reflection that is ___.
options: A concept unrelated to reflection off rough surfaces
Completely absorbed with no light bouncing at all

quiz.q: Why can you see your reflection clearly in a mirror but not in a piece of paper?
options: Mirrors do not actually reflect any light
A mirror’s smooth surface reflects light in an organized way, while paper scatters light in many directions
Paper reflects light exactly the same way a mirror does

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Persons Case and Women’s Legal Rights in Canada
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the Persons Case was a 1929 legal ruling that recognized women as “persons” eligible to serve in the Canadian Senate, marking a major milestone in the fight for women’s legal rights.

quiz.q: The Persons Case was a legal ruling that recognized women as ___.
options: “Persons” eligible to serve in the Canadian Senate
Persons only for the purpose of paying taxes
A concept unrelated to the Persons Case

quiz.q: The Persons Case was decided in ___.
options: A year unrelated to the Persons Case

quiz.q: Before the Persons Case, women were often excluded from ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the Persons Case

quiz.q: The group of women who led the legal challenge in the Persons Case became known as ___.
options: A group unrelated to the Persons Case

quiz.q: Why is the Persons Case considered a significant milestone in Canadian history?
options: The Persons Case had no effect on women’s rights
It expanded legal recognition and rights for women in Canadian public life
A reason unrelated to its historical significance
The Persons Case reduced legal rights for women

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Etymology and Word Origins
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: etymology is the study of where words come from and how their meanings have changed over time, often tracing roots back to languages such as Latin or Greek.

quiz.q: Etymology is the study of ___.
options: Where words come from and how their meanings have changed over time
The grammar rules of a language

quiz.q: Many English words trace their origins back to ___.
options: Languages such as Latin or Greek
A concept unrelated to word origins

quiz.q: Knowing a word’s Greek or Latin root can help a reader ___.
options: Determine the punctuation of a sentence
Figure out the meaning of unfamiliar related words
Guess the exact spelling of any word
A concept unrelated to word origins

quiz.q: The word biology comes from Greek roots meaning ___.
options: A meaning unrelated to the word biology

quiz.q: Why is studying etymology useful for building vocabulary?
options: Word origins never help with understanding vocabulary
Every word in English has a completely unrelated, unique origin
Recognizing common roots helps readers decode the meaning of many unfamiliar words

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Congruence and Symmetry in 2D Shapes
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: congruent shapes are identical in size and shape, and a shape has line symmetry if it can be folded along a line so both halves match exactly.

quiz.q: Two shapes are congruent if they are ___.
options: Different in both size and shape
Similar in shape but different in size

quiz.q: A shape has line symmetry if ___.
options: It can be folded along a line so both halves match exactly
It has more than four sides
It can never be folded to match on both sides
A concept unrelated to line symmetry

quiz.q: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
options: A number unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Which shape is an example of two congruent triangles placed together?
options: A rectangle divided in half diagonally
A single triangle with no division
A circle with no straight edges
A shape unrelated to congruent triangles

quiz.q: Why is understanding congruence and symmetry useful in geometry?
options: A reason unrelated to this topic
Congruence and symmetry have no practical use in geometry
All shapes are automatically congruent to one another
It helps identify relationships and patterns between shapes

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Adaptations and Natural Selection: An Introduction
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: an adaptation is a trait that helps an organism survive in its environment, and natural selection is the process by which organisms with helpful adaptations are more likely to survive and reproduce.

quiz.q: An adaptation is a trait that ___.
options: Helps an organism survive in its environment
Always harms an organism’s chances of survival
Has no effect on an organism’s survival

quiz.q: Natural selection is the process by which ___.
options: Organisms with helpful adaptations are more likely to survive and reproduce
A concept unrelated to natural selection
All organisms survive and reproduce equally, regardless of traits
Organisms lose all of their adaptations over time

quiz.q: Which is an example of a physical adaptation?
options: An object with no living characteristics
A polar bear’s thick fur for insulation in cold climates
A concept unrelated to physical adaptations
A rock sitting in a cold climate

quiz.q: A behavioural adaptation is best described as ___.
options: An action an organism takes to help it survive
A concept unrelated to behavioural adaptations
A trait with no connection to survival
A physical body part used for survival

quiz.q: Why do organisms with helpful adaptations tend to survive and reproduce more successfully over time?
options: All organisms in an environment face identical challenges regardless of traits
Adaptations never affect an organism’s chances of survival
Their traits make them better suited to overcome challenges in their environment
A reason unrelated to natural selection

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The October Crisis and the War Measures Act
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: the October Crisis of 1970 was a major political crisis centred in Quebec that led the federal government to invoke the War Measures Act, temporarily suspending certain civil liberties to respond to the emergency.

quiz.q: The October Crisis took place in ___.
options: A year unrelated to the October Crisis

quiz.q: The War Measures Act, when invoked, allowed the federal government to ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the War Measures Act
Permanently remove a province from Confederation
Temporarily suspend certain civil liberties during an emergency

quiz.q: The October Crisis was centred in the province of ___.
options: A province unrelated to the October Crisis

quiz.q: Invoking the War Measures Act during the October Crisis was controversial because it ___.
options: Limited civil liberties that Canadians normally have
Only applied to citizens outside of Canada
Had no effect on the rights of Canadian citizens
A reason unrelated to the controversy

quiz.q: Why do historians and students study the October Crisis today?
options: The October Crisis has no connection to understanding Canadian history
This event never actually took place
A reason unrelated to its historical significance
It raises important questions about balancing public safety with civil liberties during a crisis

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Identifying Cause and Effect Relationships in Text
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: identifying cause and effect in a text involves recognizing how one event or action (the cause) leads directly to a result (the effect).

quiz.q: A cause-and-effect relationship shows how ___.
options: One event leads directly to a result
A concept unrelated to cause and effect
A story’s setting connects to its characters
Two unrelated events happen at the same time

quiz.q: In the sentence “Because it rained, the game was cancelled,” the cause is ___.
options: A concept unrelated to this sentence

quiz.q: Which signal word often introduces an effect in a sentence?
options: A word unrelated to cause and effect

quiz.q: Which sentence shows a clear cause-and-effect relationship?
options: She forgot her umbrella, so she got soaked in the rain.
The umbrella and the raincoat were both new.
A sentence unrelated to cause and effect
She has a red umbrella and a blue raincoat.

quiz.q: Why is it useful for readers to identify cause-and-effect relationships in a text?
options: It helps readers understand why events happen and how ideas are connected
A reason unrelated to reading comprehension
Every event in a text happens for no reason at all
Cause-and-effect relationships never help readers understand a text

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Data: Constructing and Interpreting Stem-and-Leaf Plots
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: a stem-and-leaf plot organizes numerical data by splitting each value into a “stem” (leading digits) and a “leaf” (last digit), making it easy to see the shape and spread of a data set.

quiz.q: In a stem-and-leaf plot, the “stem” usually represents ___.
options: The leading digit or digits of a value
The final digit of a value
The total number of data points
A concept unrelated to stem-and-leaf plots

quiz.q: In a stem-and-leaf plot, the “leaf” usually represents ___.
options: The last digit of a value
A concept unrelated to stem-and-leaf plots
The leading digit of a value

quiz.q: For the value 47, which digit would typically be the stem?
options: A digit unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: One advantage of a stem-and-leaf plot is that it ___.
options: Only works with exactly two data points
Displays every individual data value while showing overall patterns
A concept unrelated to stem-and-leaf plots
Hides all of the individual data values

quiz.q: Why might a stem-and-leaf plot be useful for analyzing a set of test scores?
options: This type of plot only works with negative numbers
It shows both the exact scores and the overall distribution at a glance
Stem-and-leaf plots cannot display exact data values
A reason unrelated to analyzing data

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Ocean Currents and Their Influence on Climate
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: ocean currents are large-scale movements of seawater driven by wind, temperature, and salinity differences, and they play a major role in distributing heat and shaping climate around the world.

quiz.q: Ocean currents are large-scale movements of ___.
options: A concept unrelated to ocean currents

quiz.q: Which of these can drive ocean currents?
options: Differences in wind, temperature, and salinity
The number of fish in the ocean
A concept unrelated to ocean currents

quiz.q: Ocean currents play a major role in ___.
options: Keeping all regions of the world at the exact same temperature
Preventing any heat from moving through the ocean
A concept unrelated to ocean currents
Distributing heat and shaping climate around the world

quiz.q: A warm ocean current flowing toward a coastal region can cause that region’s climate to be ___.
options: Colder than it would otherwise be
Milder than it would otherwise be
A concept unrelated to ocean currents

quiz.q: Why do scientists study ocean currents when trying to understand global climate patterns?
options: Ocean currents move large amounts of heat around the planet, directly influencing regional climates
A reason unrelated to ocean currents
Ocean currents have no connection to climate patterns
Ocean currents only affect a single, isolated location

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Population Pyramids and Demographic Transition
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Social Studies strand: a population pyramid is a graph showing the age and sex distribution of a population, and demographic transition describes how birth and death rates change as a country develops economically.

quiz.q: A population pyramid displays a population’s distribution by ___.
options: A concept unrelated to population pyramids

quiz.q: A population pyramid with a wide base typically indicates ___.
options: A concept unrelated to population pyramids
No connection to birth rates at all
A high birth rate and a young population
A low birth rate and an aging population

quiz.q: Demographic transition describes how ___.
options: Birth and death rates change as a country develops economically
A concept unrelated to demographic transition
A country’s borders change over time

quiz.q: A country with a narrowing pyramid base over time often reflects ___.
options: A concept unrelated to population pyramids
No change in birth rates at all

quiz.q: Why are population pyramids useful tools for geographers and planners?
options: They help predict future needs for schools, healthcare, and other services
A reason unrelated to population pyramids
Population pyramids provide no useful information for planning
Population data never changes over time

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Analyzing Podcasts and Audio Storytelling
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Language strand: analyzing podcasts and audio storytelling involves considering how sound effects, music, tone of voice, and pacing are used to inform or engage a listening audience.

quiz.q: Audio storytelling relies on elements such as ___.
options: Only written text with no sound at all
A concept unrelated to audio storytelling
Sound effects, music, tone of voice, and pacing

quiz.q: A podcast host’s tone of voice can help convey ___.
options: Emotion and emphasis about the topic being discussed
Only the exact spelling of words being said
A concept unrelated to tone of voice

quiz.q: Why might a podcast use background music during a serious moment?
options: A reason unrelated to audio storytelling
To help set an emotional mood for the listener
Background music always distracts from the story with no purpose
Music never affects how a listener feels

quiz.q: Pacing in a podcast refers to ___.
options: The length of the podcast’s title
The speed and rhythm at which information is delivered

quiz.q: Why is it important to critically analyze podcasts and other audio media?
options: Critical analysis is only useful for written texts, not audio
A reason unrelated to media literacy
Audio media never influences how listeners understand a topic
It helps listeners understand how audio techniques shape their understanding of a topic

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Estimation Strategies and Rounding for Real-World Problems
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Math strand: estimation strategies, such as rounding numbers to the nearest ten or hundred, help quickly approximate answers and check whether an exact calculation is reasonable.

quiz.q: Estimation is useful because it helps ___.
options: Always produce the exact correct answer
Quickly approximate an answer and check if an exact calculation is reasonable
Replace the need for exact calculations entirely

quiz.q: Rounded to the nearest ten, 47 becomes ___.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Rounded to the nearest hundred, 342 becomes ___.
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Which situation would benefit most from an estimate rather than an exact answer?
options: Quickly checking if you have enough money for groceries
Recording an exact measurement for a science experiment
Calculating exact change owed at a cash register

quiz.q: Why is it useful to estimate before solving a problem exactly?
options: Estimating always gives a more accurate result than exact calculation
Estimating never helps confirm whether an answer makes sense
It helps identify whether the final exact answer is reasonable

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Scientific Method: Variables and Designing a Fair Test
subjects.summary: Grade 7 Science strand: a fair test changes only one variable at a time while keeping all other conditions constant, allowing scientists to determine the true cause of a result.

quiz.q: A fair test changes ___.
options: A concept unrelated to a fair test
Every variable at the same time
Only one variable at a time

quiz.q: The variable that a scientist deliberately changes in an experiment is called the ___.
